Достаточно комфортный аэропорт.Недавно открыли в зоне вылета что-то похожее на Duty Free.Большой и требует инвестиций

Airport without car is only reachable by bus from closest train station and does not offer much to do  so be aware to not arrive well ahead of time. Wifi is strong  security and checkin quite quickly. Boarding may be slow if another aircraft is already boarding.

Fantastic small airport. Check-In and Security cleared within minutes. Gate 1 offered good views across the airfield  but limited seating  due to some form of construction work taking place. Public transport access to the airport seems rather limited though  with either a 10-15min walk from Mortsel Station  or a taxi ride from Antwerp City.

Great little airport  very fast. Fokker 50heaven ;) Parking is still free but this will change soon with the expansion of flights going on. The home carrier VLM is a fantastic airline as well! Premium service for a low cost price !

As we were flying with a private plane from this airport  the facilities were of course ecellent. No time waiting  private check-in and security check  great lounge... When you're flying on a regular flight  the aiport is very convenient as well. Small  but fast. It's mainly a business airport so good food and WiFi  but less shops.

Well  it's a really small airport with bad connections to the city... Atleast the customs were fast and friendly  also the walking from my plane (ATR72) to the terminal was kinda nice  ah well  that's because I was surrounded by maybe 5 fokker 50's! XD But  from what I saw  it's just another small airport  nothing special.
This is a great little airport and if you have the time they have a super aircraft museum right next door.
The only one airport I was with toilets for money only... It is kind a joke... really bad experience!
It is a joke that you have to pay for the toilet on the airport. I have never experienced something like that crazy idea!

This smaller  regional  airport has undergone significant improvements since its last construction work in 2022. It does not provide a drop and go zone  requiring passengers to utilize the main parking area and pay a fee of 3€ for entry and exit  which could be considered relatively expensive. The parking facilities were quite full during my visit. Despite being crowded  the security procedures were executed swiftly and efficiently. The airport's installations were well-maintained and clean  leaving no room for complaints in that department.
The price of the official car parks has been raised again this month (July 2023). Where I used to pay 27 € I now have to pay 35 € : a 25 % raise !
Even toilettes (upon arrival) are paid! Despite each passenger pays airport fees within the ticket price. outrageous

Very unsexy Airport. Chaotig facilities and parking situation. Food court available but extremely narrow and crowded. Much to less seats at the gates. Leaving the flight within rain is a mess  complete foodway hasnt a roof. Signs for parking quite rare. Even if booked a parking f.e. at P2 you have to circle and circle to find a parking lot or wait until someone leaves. Trashy.
A bus stop in the middle of nowhere has more to offer than this excuse of an airport. I guess there must be worse  but for a European airport  this is scraping the bottom.

An airport that does not pretend to be something it is not. Despite its small size immigration was fast and baggage reclaim was also swift. Getting to Brussels is easy enough  however  the bus can get busy and you may need to wait for the next one.
Well its typical ryanair territory  like 40 min away from the city  other than that its well equipped clean and fresh  with friendly staff

It’s a really good airport  check in was easy to find and quick to get through as well as security and they had a good few restaurants and a big duty free. They had a lovely little terrace with a bar  which would be lovely in summer!
Great little airport  handles a handful of flights a day and has a relatively busy terminal  which it can handle.

It took very  very long for the bagage belt to start moving. The first people collected their bagage 10 minutes after they arrived at the belt. After the bagage claim it was really strait forward as with all journeys within the EU.
Not a too long walk and quick check-in and security. Enough seating in the gate area and a few shops for food and leisure items.
A long walk  too busy check-in area and very limited seating in the gate area. The COVID-19 check also took very long.

BRUSSELS SOUTH CHARLEROI AIRPORT IS A PRETTY EFFICIENT LOW-COST AIRPORT. HOWEVER  GETTING TO THE AIRPORT FROM CHARLEROI-SUD TRAIN STATION IS VERY PAINFUL. TEC'S BUSES BRINGS VERY POOR SERVICES (BUS THAT DOES NOT COME AND/OR SEVERAL TEMPORARY STATION AROUND THE AIRPORT NOT DISPLAYED AND CHANGING OFTEN). I DID NOT TRIED FLIBCO BUSES THAT SEEMS TO BRING BETTER SERVICES DIRECTLY FROM ZUID STATION IN BRUSSELS AND OTHER CITY CENTER IN BELGIUM. OBVIOUSLY THESE SERVICES ARE OFFERED AT A HIGHER COST.

The airport is somewhat hard to find. Many roadsigns leading to the airport are actually wrong. Use a GPS or Google Maps  don't follow the road signs. Friendliness of staff is horrible. Airport staff (especially security  but also some PSA's) are really unfriendly. Not just a bit  but a lot. Like there is an internal competition going on who can be the unfriendliest. I've noticed this on previous occasions  but thought I was unlucky with the persons working on that day. This is the third time I fly from this airport and I was really impressed by how unfriendly a person can be. Mainly for this reason I can absolutely not recommend Charleroi Airport.
Nice airport with limited things to do in it as it is small. WiFi is not great as you have to pay for it so I did not use it at all.

Brussels Charleroi is a small compact airport only marked down for me as it is a fair way from Brussels Central (47kms)  which means you really need to factor that in for early departures. Also since the attack at the main Brussels Airport  security means nobody pulls up out front of the terminal  you are dropped some way off then walk through a tent like structure as an initial security barrier normally full of heavily armed security personnel  then you walk across to the terminal doors over the old road that has long since been blocked off. Has good amenities and not bad  just let down by the access and distance for me.

We bought train tickets to Brussels after landing near midnight at the ticket vending machine only to know that the trains stop commuting after 22:30. Though there is a bus of the same price and we get to the city with no problem  we were not able to easily refund those train tickets.

Very little space  not enough seating area  narrow halls. slow wi-fi and not that good public trasport to town. Tickets can be purchased only with card or coins! Not bills accepted  so if you travel further there is no way that you would have 30euro in coins! This is sick.
Terminal 2 has only one bar/cafeteria. Too small for 3 gates (around 600 people in  at the same time).
Way too long at the security check  we almost missed our flight due to the queue. Otherwise it's basic low cost airport  quite operational.

Super dirty in every single spot (even the restaurant part). A huge amount of people - totally not prepared for the number of passengers. One of the worst airports I've had a chance to use. For plus - they finally made free wi-fi access. Generally - not recommending.

Immigration was at its worst this evening. Some 200 pax packed in a low-ceilinged room  hot  stuffy  with only two immigration officers on duty (later  they woke up two more). But it took me a good 20 minutes to get through. Charleroi is no more the smart and friendly airport when you could go in or out in a few minutes ... its huge success has changed all that for the worst.
Overcrowded  especially in the rush hours  dirty lounges and often dirty bathrooms  ridiculously overpriced Internet connection (still!)  overpriced food and water... A total mess...
Charleroi is victim of its success : always crowded  queues at the security check  not a lot of breathing space anywhere !

Charleroi  8 millions of passengers per year  but the terminal is not designed for this capacity. Getting to the airport is not bad  but there is no possibility to get to the airport without paying entrance fees. The bus costs 6€  parking form 0 to 10 minutes costs 50 cents  no way out to the bus which stops a street further and costs 3€. Security terrible  they throw with your belongings  don't speak english  dutch  only french or arabic. When sth falls on the ground they say "it's not me"  it could be your computer  phone  doesn't matter  they don't care about anything. No free wifi  15 minutes around 2€ so for nowadays it very expensive. Drinks beginning from 3€ and up  food from 5€. Terrible  that is what I can say and it's not particularly this one time  I have flown from this airport like 100 times and every time even worse.

A small and crowded airport but it manages to get all the passengers from a to b in a decent manner. Getting to the airport is only acceptable by car or a busride from charleroi which takes 30 minutes. They are expanding the airport so hopefully the future will bring more terminal facilities.
One of the worst airports in Europe right now  overcrowded  often dirty  and with an expensive Internet connection (yes  in 2016 we still need to pay for Internet in the center of Europe).

C'est un aéroport low-cost  base de Ryanair. Ne pas s'attendre à du grand luxe. Mais le dimensionnement pourrait être revu pour le contrôle sécurité  pour les halls souvent noirs de monde. Le parking est très cher  ils doivent se rattraper là-dessus
It was fucking fuck There was NO transport to get to the Charleroi-city Also there was too less space for passengers And fucking non-free wifi

The security was the worst experience  very impolite staff  unhelpful as they were for a family of six trying to navigate through customs. The food was the most overpriced I've ever seen  the water could be termed as price-gouging.
Late night arrival  no food available. Shuttle service was ready to take us to the nearby hotel. Pretty deserted and not altogether well-marked.

Well  in one of the reviews someone wrote "It was fucking fuck". I prefer not to use such language  but I do agree. The airport itself is actually pretty ok - on arrival I got out of the airport quickly  and on the way back I came 15 minutes before departure and still made the flight (including security check  border control and all of that stuff). But the fact I had to pay almost 100€ for 2 people for a return bus ticket to Brussels  by a very average bus that felt like it was going 60 km/h  makes me never want to fly to this airport again. Never.

Charleroi was a small airport and everything for claiming our luggage to checkin in upon arrival for our flight back was quick  smooth and easy. The staff was friendly and my overall impression of the airport was efficient  smooth and clean.
Incredibly inconvenient for overnight transfers. I coyuldn't even but water  as all cafes are closed  and machines accept coins only.

The facilities of CRL are ok  but the problem is that the amount of travellers is growing at a crazy rate. The result is a terminal that feels cramped and crowded all the time. Especially arriving in the late evening is a horrible experience  as navigating the enormous amounts of people takes quite some effort. Plus the lack of free wifi is appaling in a Western European airport. Transport options are ok as there are frequent and affordable shuttle buses to Brussels South train station. No free wifi here  either  though.

Very expensive and overcrowded airport  far away from the city with expensive bus connection having a monopoly on the route. There is no free wifi. I hate Charleroi airport from my very heart and soul!

If you buy your ticket in advance at flibco.com  you can get to Lille from the airport for €5. WiFi is to be payed. At the restaurant 1 bottle of water (0.5l) costs €3.4  at the newspaper shop it costs €2.

Charleroi is a small airport caring mainly for LCC (Ryanair has one of its main base at CRL Wizzair connects it with destinations in Eastern Europe). It's cheerful  and pleasant  but it can be crowded at times;

The airport is located about an hour of distance from Brussels  however  the capital is easily accessible by a large amount of buses. Basically  the airport facilities are specious and well-kept. Unfortunately  there's NO free Wi-Fi available at the airport  as well as the amount of energy sockets is limited.

The airport is actually quite far from Brussels. The transport connections are awful. Still there is no need to complain because the plane tickets are so cheap ! The airport is known for receiving illegal regional funding (according to EU) so let's profit. Quite practical to go and spend its money under the sun (because let's face it Belgium is not a sunny place ;-)

I flew to Charleroi for 6 hours from Budapest.I dont have any problem with airport but city center and train connection is very tragic.Everybody waited 45 minutes for bus on outside and weather is very cold too.I will be in city center later 2 hours from Charleroi.I never fly this airport for Brussels

The most horrible airport I have ever seen. Slow and bad Xray control. There was no free wifi but an expensive one (15 mins- 2 euro). Boarding at the gate for Trapani was unfair because a lot of people got into the priority line without having a priority ticket. Furthermore  they didn't even check what was written on that paper. I don't reccomend to anybody that airport and wish the flight assistants were awaken.

Der ordentliche Bereich ist lächerlich klein. Nach der Sicherheitskontrolle wird es angenehmer und etwas ruhiger. Schöner Blick durch große Glasscheiben auf das Vorfeld. Für Flüge außerhalb der EU gibt es einen kleinen Duty Free Shop.

Very small and busy airport. Very long queues to sercurity check but sercuritty crew were OK. NO free Wi-Fi at all! Some shops on terminal but nothing special. Typical low-fares airlines airport. Overall 4/5 ;)

You don't have to be there really much in advance. One hour before flight departure is more than enough. The airport itself is tiny and there are not many shops. There is Wi-Fi but you have to pay a tremendous amount of money for it.

Quite good airport  a lot of possibilities to travel to Luxembourg/ France/ Belgium using cheap buses outside the terminal. Wi-Fi isn't free  stores are great. You can easily charge your phone using power sockets.

Small  but very useful airport. Easy to catch from Brussels and all Belgium by train or by bus. Nice restaurants and good foods  beers. Very clean  and open 24 hours. You can see the planes from the terminal building. Wifi isn't free  very expensive  without an account you can only see the airport website.

Charleroi Brussels South is a fine airport  by regional airports standards. There are a few shops for goodies  food and drinks  but it's nothing impressive. Things usually go quicker than in bigger airports. When we arrived back here from our trip  we did not have to wait for our luggage at all. It's also a nice looking airport. What makes it feel a little cheap though  is the fact that you have to walk outside to board your plane. Also  wifi is not free.
A nice low-cost airport  easy and convenient; nice people  too.
15 euro cost taxi/bus for one person to the Brussels. Taxi drivers are catching every single person who is leaving the airport.
Good airport - Lots of Duty free for purchases - But the control was disorganized - For planespotters  you have good places to see the airplanes

Worst airport in Europe. No space for security check  food is crap  no place to sit at the gates  too crowded even outside holliday season. When arriving there is a parking is terrible and blocked constantly.
Small  crowded  and dirty. Behind Bucharest Baneasa  the worst airport I've ever flown to/from.
Small but well organized airport.
Great and efficient airport !
Small and crowded
very good

Long queues to security control  beacuse of only 2 checkpoints open and a lot of ads saying you can pay 4€ for less crappy security check. Almost 20 minutes spend on crowded stairs before finally boarding the plane.
small but got a few good spot to sleep :D slept there for 3nights in a row :D
Horrible airport  poor service
Horrible accessibility from anywhere besides Charleroi itself.
brussels south is an good airport  the only bad thing is the chekpoint before you get to the gates where they check your handluggage

Very good low cost airport  but very small and on rush hours very busy. Strict rules. Clean and not croudy. Few shops but good shops. You can reach Brussels only with a shuttle bus because it's quite far.
This airport exceeded my expectations for such a small airport. It's modern and clean  and not too busy. However  since it's such a small airport there barely are shops or restaurants.
It's a cheap airport. Far away from Brussels and 15 min from Charleroi. Basic facilities  but clean and good info.

Disaster. Dirty bathrooms  staff with personality issues (excepting the security staff which is quite professional and correct)  expensive (10 EUR for a blackened burger with a card board bun). Food court out of stock after 7PM.
Good airport  friendly staff but it's far from Brussels: with train 60 min and a return ticket is 10 Euro.

(-) est devenu petit... alors qu'il vient d'être construit (-) parking infernal (+) passage de douane par contre = le plus aimables et les plus gentils de tous les aéroports que j'ai utilisé (un bon paquet!)
Not bad  just far from Brussels if your flight is at 6:50 a.m. because it is difficult to have a bus so early.

Brussels Airport stands out as a prime example of a well-equipped and comfortable airport  offering an array of shops and services that cater to a diverse range of needs and preferences. The terminal's layout and amenities are designed to provide a pleasant experience for travelers. The array of retail outlets and service points is impressive  ensuring that passengers have access to everything they might need before their flight. From last-minute gifts to travel essentials  the shopping experience at Brussels Airport is both convenient and enjoyable. However  one area where the airport could improve is in its provision of VIP lounges  especially those accessible through the LoungeKey program. While the existing lounges offer a degree of comfort and exclusivity  the limited options for LoungeKey members mean that many travelers are unable to take advantage of this facility. The addition of more VIP lounges compatible with LoungeKey would significantly enhance the overall experience for frequent travelers and those looking for an extra layer of comfort and service. In summary  Brussels Airport excels in terms of its shopping and general services  providing a comfortable and efficient environment for travelers. However  the expansion of VIP lounge offerings  particularly for LoungeKey members  would be a welcome improvement  elevating the airport's status as a top-tier travel hub.

Mostar Airport  a small  pleasent and friendly airport. Perfect posititon  20km from Medugorje and 60km from Adriatic Sea. Check out Mistral Air and Meridiana fly web pages to get flights from Italy to Mostar. Visit www.mostar-airport.ba for more informations.

Mostar Airport was opened for civilian air traffic in 1965 for domestic flights. In 1984  the airport became Mostar International Airport and was announced to be an alternative for Sarajevo International Airport during the 1984 Winter Olympics. The airport is also popular for people making the pilgrimage to nearby Međugorje. In 2012  the company had a twofold increase in traffic  making it the second busiest in the Bosnia and Herzegovina Kindly persons at Mostar International airport and very nice City of Mostar. Be sure to visit.

Only thing missing is strong domestic Bosnian air carrier to bring some competitions to Star Alliance airlines with still high air ticket prices to Sarajevo. Hopefully Bosnian Wand Airlines will become strong player at Sarajevo Airport.

One of the easiest airports to get through everything is right there  no hassle  no long waits. Access is easy even when several flights come in at the same time (which is rare). One of the best small airports around. Its only problem is the fog (winter months).

Very nice airport.It easy to navigate you can't get lost even if you want to.Nice scenery during approach and takeoff Big parking infront of the airport.You can pick up your baggage in no time after passing customs check.
Very nice small Airport

I don't know if it can get any worse. I understand that you have to expand and that it can create problems but that employees are so melancholy and almost angry cannot be explained. Improvement is needed on all points
the good thing about tuzla airport is that there is an airport...nothing else... catastrophical apocalyptical place...

I had to wait 30 minutes outside under the rain because of the slow passport control. But the worst is that the flight from Sweden that landed only a few minutes after us had to wait until we have finished with the passport control to leave the plane. My flight from Vienna to Tuzla took 40 minutes but passport control took my about 55 minutes at Tuzla airport itself! This is getting worst from year to year...
Airport is being renovated but as the works are being done  it is now overcrowded and it is desastruous! I hope that the new terminal will be better.

Airport has everyhting you need. two giftshops two restourants and caffes   two gates  and there is going to be built a new building much larger this year  so capacity is going to increase. There is wifi connection and food is brilliant. It is 15 km from Tuzla and 8km from Živinice towns

Very small airport. Wizz Air is the only operating airline here. There are plenty car rental desks  and shuttle services. Only 2 check-in desks operating  but they handle 1-2 flight at a time only as well. There is a bar upstairs  with view to the apron. They serve food  drinks and coffee :) No Wi-Fi. Very friendly  welcoming staff. Passport controll is only 2 desks  and terribly slow. Welcome to BH :)
Beautiful new and modern airport  easy to get to and easy to navigate. Security is a breeze but there are no services once you pass through security and only limited options anywhere in the airport.

The airport is in 10 minutes walking distance from the beach  awesome! But there is not enough sitting area inside the terminal  especially because WizzAir has a whole gate area reserved and they don't let you in  only during boarding.
An absolute rip-off. Even by airport standards the prices in this airport were extortionate. €7 for 6 nuggets! This is in a country where the average wage is far lower than Western Europe.

A really small airport. It is nice outside the tourist season but in summer it is a nightmare. Checkin employees work slow  big queues for the security check  rude security officers. Be prepared for a long wait and try to keep your nerve.
That’s one of the best airports I’ve been  I mean the customer service is top I actually didn’t expect it.
Very impressing! Immigrations/customs were very fast to get to. Wifi wasn’t really good. No food places  only a Bulgarian old resturant.

My flight was cancelled and next flight was delayed but the staff at the airport did not give any information about what was going on. They were completely incompetent and this should not happen at the international airport.

Badly managed airport. They had 8 flights on that day and their managers decided to make 4 of them at the same time.So when you land prepare yourself to wait at least 20-30 mins to clear the immigration.Also their duty free is not duty free as most of the items are 2 times more expensive from every normal high street shop.

This airport is shame for Fraport. Only junk food restaurant's the air conditioner been turned off and the on that day was only 4-5 flights but the security check's takes ages as their staff is unexperienced.
A new passenger terminal has nothing in common with what it was before. Pleasant and comfortable place. Has a special place for smoking after check-in.

JThis airport in 1991 was the worst airport imaginable. Food and drink was very scarce. The airport was filthy and I was grabbed by security and taken into a room to be searched. I was manhandled and treated like a criminal. I had nothing I was not supposed to have. The whole experience was a nightmare. I guess things have relaxed nowadays with Communism long gone. But at the time it had only just ended.

Poskładany po zatruciu pokarmowym  kroplówka na lotnisku o 5 rano. Cała drogę z hotelu wymioty i biegunka. Poskładany po zatruciu pokarmowym  kroplówka na lotnisku o 5 rano. Cała drogę z hotelu wymioty i biegunka.

An average airport. Upon arrival you were "forced" to stand in a enormous line (filled with Russians) because they couldnøt be bothered to open extra custom/immigration points. Absolutely no free wifi to find anywhere on the premises  and frankly  the food selection is absolutely not recommended.

Wi-fi in departure zone is not working  and on-line check-in is not available  parking is very small  hoses for aircrafts are absent - and this all is in new airport building!!! Cannot buy magnets un Duty-Free
Small old but clean airport. McDonalds in terrace.
The airport may not be the most beautiful and fascinating airport but it works as it should!
Ugly airport
Not to big airport but not bad  long time must wait for bagage.
Dlugi czas oczekiwania na wydanie bagażu.
Not the best for departing  the lounge is to crowded  and its old fashioned  but its ok for an eastern European airport
small  nice airport  no problems
very small and old airport but nice
Długie oczekiwanie na wydanie bagażu z luku bagażowego.
Not bad as airport. Like the view on the runways. Hate long lines before registration(((
it wasn't just small it was very crowded as well. you had to try not to step on people. because it was very over crowded and over the Bulgarian heat it was very hot
yes small
small
small airport
Skvělá vyhlídka
Waited 1hr 15mins for our bags.
Don't have ATM  Dont work any shop  don't have fud Court  .... If you coming in this airport take with you Food and water.
No public transport (taxis affordable though) and someone smoked in the airside restrooms. Single tiny shop airside  small cafe/kiosk outside. Efficient to move through.
No public transport to the airport at all  only taxi. No wifi  one cafe that had sold out of food. Not a very good experience here.
Very small airport with average facilities  a bit limited the choice of food services  but nothing is bad.
Lovley scenic and hastle free airport
Terrible facilities. However take note this was 2006 so things may (hopefully) have improved here!
Wizz uses Terminal 1. Very old building. Overall ok. Toilets could be cleaner Metro connects only to Terminal 2. Shuttle bus every 30 min. Public transport also via Bus available
Sofia airport was very simple and easy to pass through. Just a short queue at customs. The bags took a while to come through at baggage claim.

I have not flown from Terminal 1 since long ago  but check-in and security check were fast and flawless. The queue at the gate was however long and the flight was delayed for 1:25 hours  so not the best experience.
Terminal 2 is great  but Terminal 1 is 70 years old and only Wizz air and some times Easy jet fly from there.

A bit hard to check in  but the terminal was nice and clean. Security was a bit long and tedious  but once you get past it  the gates are right there. No duty-free you have to walk through. The yellow metro line connects the city of Sofia to the airport in around 20 minutes to half an hour. Not a great selection of shops and restaurants in the newer terminal.
The domestic gate (D1) has nothing more than a vending machine  overpriced pavilion and a few places to sit. There isn't even a toilet after passing the security check!

Really too expensive to eat there. 16.5 BGN (8 4€) for a sandwich  8 BGN (4 1€) for a 500ml bottle of water!! Way overpriced. It's not normal that I can buy a good bottle of wine for 8€ at the Duty Free and  immediately after  I pay more than 21€ for 2 sandwiches and a small water!
Getting to and from the airport is preferable with metro  but thera also busses and taxis. Sequrity check is faster with passport.

At first it wasn't clear that I had to be at the other terminal (which is 3km further). There was a free shuttle  but the diesel van caused for a headache as engine was running while waiting to leave. For the rest very good airport with nice scenery
Very poor lounge. Only cold side dishes. No fast track on security control. No fast track on boarding. Business class paxes must wait in queue with all of People.

Getting to the airport is easy by bus  car  metro (my favourite)  or taxi  but be careful of taxi's rates. Terminal 2 has good facilities but needs more various food and retail services. WiFi is good and there are nice USB outlets and power for phones  laptops  and other mobile devices. WiFi is very good. Security check could be slow during peak hours  but otherwise is quick and troublesome. There are electronic passport terminals for departures and arrivals.
Significant improvement is noticeable last few months. More options for meal  charging points and e.t.c
Tenho poucas lembranças do aeroporto de Sófia mas lembro de achar o aeroporto simpático. Infelizmente não poderei avaliar da melhor forma possível.

Terminal 1 is absolutely appalling  in total disrepair and lack of cleanliness. Its biggest advantage is it’s small and manageable  but feels extremely crowded and lacks adequate seating at peak times. The new cafés are ok  but this is just polishing the turd. Needs to be closed  and frankly the end of next year date that’s been set is not soon enough given how bad it is.

At Sofia Airport the Border Police officer was so nice and kind  even made joke and made you comfortable even you’re tired from the night flight  it was really nice and I didn’t expect it  but it did made me smile. The only thing is that they still check if you’ve got vaccines or tests when they said they’ve removed it on 1st of April.

Getting to the airport is fantastically easy via Metro  under 40 minutes from Serdika. However  if arriving late then Taxi services are available. Would like it if they at least had late night buses. Went through security and check-in like a breeze. The wifi was absolutely dreadful  at least they had nice and workable toilets.

Basically you paid to be with priority  but passengers without priority were checking in quicker and for them were opened 2 desks also after that they board you on the same bus so I don’t see any point of that! Also the airport staff are tough ????

Terminal 1. Dirty  ugly  cold and stuck in 50s! Rolls on security check are even rusty. From this terminal flies one who must fly from there. Security told me to pass even I forgot 500ml of beer can in my hand baggage. Thank you for beer  but... Bad for EU.
Turkish Airlines does not staff its check-in desks so the experience was substandard and a little chaotic. It took over one hour to check in the flight.
Not a good airport  but the biggest one in Bulgaria. Terminal 1 is perhaps the worst one in Europe  but while T2 is better  by no means is it good.
When you pass through security there is no toilets no nothing  just seats and the flight was delayed we had to get back to the terminal to get anything and pass through security again.
Although I am Bulgarian  I can see the difference in the various airports. No WiFi in the arrival terminal  very slow passport checking.

The greatest plus is easy access from the city centre by Metro. The lounge is realy basic compared to other airports  even inthe region and wireless is not working well either  it stops from time to time.

A fairly comfortable and spacious airport with a convenient metro connection to the city  although this applies to Terminal 2 only since I have no experience of Terminal 1. It would be boring for a transit layover though since there isn't much to do on the airside other than buy souvernirs or sit in overpriced cafes. Also  the seating capacity at the gates is ridiculously bad.

Awful drop luggage experience  boarding pass control and one of the worst border pass control. While barely sitting down to use one of the fewest sockets available  been told that the full section of that area will be closed!!!

This is about Terminal 1  as Terminal 2 is much better. Terminal 1 is quite old-fashioned and is only used y low-cost airlines. People at security check are extremely rude and the whole system is very inefficient.
The old terminal 1 is awful. You may end up waiting on immigration for 30+ minutes. The baggage reclaim belt gets stuck with bags of people who haven't passed immigration yet.
Very good Metro connection to the airport. Security time too long. Only 2 open counters. Aspire Lounge average.

Terminal 1 - they had only 1 from 2 security controls opened. Because of that  there was a long queue for security...and after that  incredibly  they closed one from two security belts running on this security desk. If you have a flight shortly  that don't care. Simply  they need a break - if there are many people or not.

Passport control extremely slow and unable to cope with the arriving passengers at acceptable time  although serving only one air company - Wizz air. You get additional 10 % discount at the duty free on arrival.

Sofia airports has two terminals : T1 (WizzAir) is rather old  T2 (Ryanair) is modern. A very convenient metro just next to T2 T2  half an hour to the city center  cost is 1 60 lev (0 80 €). A shuttle bus (free) from T1 to T2  but only every half hour.
Was like stepping back in time  Terrible Airport. Just wondering what it will be like departing from there.

Plan your transfer to and from the airport well in advance. Public transport - Bus 84 goes to the city center. Overpriced cafes. Airport authorities ask people to arrive 2 hrs before flight departure for security reasons.

Very small airport. No smoking area ( exp. business lounge). Poor food and retail services - only 2 shops and 2 small points with food and beverages. You can take subway direct to city centre or with transfer to business park - it's a good option

Terminal 1 at Sofia is a disaster. Five flights leaving  only four security belts available and open. Equipment kept breaking down. It took me 90 minutes after checking in to get through security and passport control to the gate.

Sofia International Airport is a pretty airport in the area of the capital of Bulgaria and it consists of 2 terminal  an old and a new one  with a car park. I have never seen the old terminal but the new terimnal is clean  clear and quiet comfortable. The airport staff is very friendly but bit slow. Also the flights aren't always on time. The food is good but nothings special. So on the whole it's a great airport!

Sofia is my hometown so Sofia airport is always my point of departure. It is a small one and very compact and easy to find everything inside. I suggest you use a taxi to come to the airport as the public transport is not always on time or is too crowded  however you should try to avoid getting a taxi when you arrive as the prices are way too high departing from the airport area. Make sure you come to the airport with full stomach as there are not many places you can get decent food. The prices in the duty free shop on Arrivals are pretty good  so don't loose the chance to stop by and do some shopping. Enjoy your stay / Have a safe flight! :)

No Fast Track for Business Class in Terminal 2. Confusing layout and very slow security took almost 30 minutes to process 50 people. Even though this is the new terminal its very confusing to find your gates such as A1 is hidden to the left and behind a lot of shops.

To be fair first of all  I have only been to Terminal 1. I heard T2 is much better  but T1 is horrible. It looks like an old bus station in a former Soviet country. Very low quality food and drink services  and very low quality security control. What I did like a lot were the wall mural maps of Bulgaria and Europe - those are really nice!

Not very crowded  and good  free WiFi are definitely a plus! The airport however is a bit boring  and the restaurants are not really restaurants  but bars offering sandwiches only. Shopping is relatively poor. Again  on the plus side  the immigration and security is fast in T2

Terminal 1 - old  crappy and pending demolition/refurbishment soon. Used by low-cost airlines. Terminal 2 - new  very convenient and FAST terminal where you'd fly through passport control and the gate. Security is tighter than in most of the other EU airports  but is still a quick and painless few-minutes-only exercise. Food at the airport is almost not present... But hey  would you be even thinking of having a meal at the airport when you have some of the best and cheap food in the world awaiting you at every corner in tasty Bulgaria?!:)
Baggage slow
It's fantastic luxary bulgarian aiport.
Terminal 1 - very old  but I loved it. Was my first flight abroad :)
I hate Terminal 1. Check-in and security is slow.
Crappy 1st terminal
Amazing surroundings. Beautiful airport. Great for planespotters. Free wifi. Cheap drinks and food.
Not the best airport but for Eastern Europe is not bad.
SOF is like my extra home. I like it!
Terminal 2 is very nice  clean  but havent good restaurant  bar  shop....
Terminal 2 is clean  warm and pleasant. The "sterile" zone shines. The staff is extremely friendly  polite and helping to all for everything. This is one of my favorite European airports.
no good resturant at departures and arrivals lobby:( j was waiting 5 hours for next plane - nothing to do at this airport. ther is no luggage store too.
Calm and relaxing airport.
Too much security.
dont remember
very good landing
sehr übersichtliches neues Terminal 2
At the old terminal. I liked it more than the one now
IT WAS SAD TO BE IN ALL THE AIR PORT EVERONE IS POOR AND ASK FOR MONEY IN 2017 AND THE TERMINAL IS NOT GOOD AT ALL
Very small and nice airport. It has some restaurants and shops. If you are waiting for 30 minutes it's perfect
Много чисто и поддържано летище. Всичко работи и става бързо.
Little  but nice airport. There is McDonalds inside and some cozy cafes  also there is a little terrace outside after check-in.

L'aeroporto migliore che abbia mai visto. Pulito  ampio  non affollato. L'autobus 409 porta dritto in città e si prende esattamente davanti all'aeroporto. Il bar con la terrazza sulle piste è un tocco di classe.
It's home! Also  it has a brand new main terminal building which is quite good for the overall standards in this area of the world.

terrible service: long-awaited ramp to the plane  then the apron waiting for a bus  three officers at passport control and at the same time  still had to wait for luggage and a pram longer. Airport service to send to the client-course! ужасный сервис: долго ждали трап к самолету  потом ждали перронный автобус  три сотрудника на паспортном контроле  и при этом  еще пришлось ждать багаж  а детскую коляску еще дольше. службы аэропорта отправить на курсы клиентоориентированности!

appalling service: ruthless examination to be asleep in the stroller infants and baby food in hand luggage; confusion at the gate; not enough seats in the styryl zone; Paid baggage carts. Airport staff aggressively tuned - this was not even on the inspection to Dubai

I am really missing the terrace  like it was in the old terminal. During the early 90s there was a pastry shop with public access and one could watch planes while there. There is now an old "abandoned" Tupolev 154M (reg. LZ-MIV)  which is getting rustier every day. It should be put outside the fence  just next to the entrance of the new terminal.

From what I remember it is rather old. We've waited for our baggage for a really long time even when there were no other planes landing. The departure zone was quite ok  but the toilets were dirty and small. There is a nice open terrace. Generally ok
I cant remember it...
This a relatively small airport  but all-in-all the service was okay.
Terrible airport and staff. Big you not boarding announcements in English  one gate  not boarding and check in screens. No airconditioning. Lots of security screening. Not WiFi
Per essere in Burundi va bene!
Most propably one of the worse airport in the world. never change since I have been travlling in Africa (27 years)

One of the worst airports with no minimal facilities for the passengers. No wifi at all  no food services and moreover the customs officers and police they always try to make trouble with the travellers by asking uncessesary questions and no proper sign boards in the airport.
Транзит. Пока идёшь тебя останавливают через каждые 30 метров для проверки документов. Такое ощущение  что все служащие аэропорта только и заняты проверкой этих самых документов))
Вылетал один рейс  регистрация в одно окно  очередь на два часа. Концертов нет  на улице и в здании +30.

I am shocked to see children playing football beside the runway with NO FENCE or such thing to prevent them from entering. There is a huge Tent neighbourhood adjacent to the airport and NO wall  NO Fence  NOTHING between.. Pickup trucks are driving around the runway to "stop people" from running into the strip while takeoff/landing. No idea how International airlines fly to Bangui with these inregularities in terms of airport security... This should be investigated.
Quite a large regional airport at the southern corner of croatia. It has everything you may possibly need  albeit the offerings may be a bit on the expensive side.

Getting to the airport was easy  with a shuttle bus. The airport doesn't accept mobile boarding passes (but they accept regular boarding pass shown on a phone). Security check was disorganised  it took us 40 minutes to pass security  the queue was super long and 2 queues merged together in chaos. The terminal facilities are OK  but there are almost none food options. there were only two cafés selling cold salads and baguettes (they didn't even heat it - they have no ovens). Lounge is open to only business class flyers and does not accept any lounge passes. Besides  boarding was disorganised as well - it seemed like the airport didn't communicate properly with the airline.
Dubrovnik airport was a very disappointed experience! What an expensive airport for anything and the services are equivalent to low cost airport.
It was a very small but well organized airport with one exception  they mixed 3 flights at the same luggage claim and it was a bit messy

A small airport easy to navigate. Security was fast. Not much in the way of refreshments available and like all airports they are overpriced. Airport is a fair distance from Dubrovnik and taxies are expensive but an airport bus is available and relatively frequent. Arrivals might involve small queue at immigration
The airprot was very tiny and crowded. Long queues and lots of noise everywhere. Did not really enjoy the design or how the airprot is built.
Nice small airport although getting bigger but security check fast. Airport though is a long way from Dubrovnik and costs in the region of Euro35-40 in taxi to get there
Most shops closed in winter. Wifi only free for 30 min. If passengers checked in online  they cannot print boarding pass at automatic machine.

The airport is about the same size as Doncaster Sheffield (Robin Hood) Airport  however due to the great amount of passengers the airport is very crowded  and not what one could call a relaxed atmosphere.

Check-in was super fast and stuff was so friendly. Security check was also fast even it was so detailed. Terminal facilities is so good for small airport like this one in Dubrovnik; good wifi  comfy chairs  food is also good ...
Many times you have to go to the plane by bus. Immigration is very quick. The baggage arrives fast o the belt.

The airport got almost empty in the end of the morning  no other flight being scheduled 1h before or after mine. Therefore  security checks were very fast  but the domestic departure side is small and the only 2 shops were closed when I first arriver 2 hours before departure.

A complete zoo from rental car return to check in chaos to boarding. Lovely city  gorgeous country  kind people in general  but the airport was very disorganised. Check in opened 75 minutes prior to original flight time. Then flight delayed 40 minutes.

No drinking water fountains available so that they can charge £3 for a bottle of water (BA only charges £1.80). "Last call" was announced before boarding time  panicked then had to wait 10 minutes in a bus - BA is not important enough to warrant one of their air-bridges.
New terminal as well  easy to navigate  staff is polite and they cooperate in every problem. There is no more croud of people waiting... Thumb up!

Easy simple airport  although not much choice of eating places. Food was nice but no option for a hot meal. Airport was easy to use though. Some departure gates (those that use a jetty) are upstairs  and bus departure gates are downstairs.

Good well laid out airport on top of a hill. Easy to get to from where we were staying in Cavtat but a bit of a trek if you're staying in Dubrovnik. That said  the drive is very scenic  or  if you really want to arrive into Dubrovnik in style take a boat from Cavtat! Buses to and from aircraft are large and hold plenty of people. Terminal is clean and well presented  and the Croatian people are friendly and accommodating.

Nice and clean airport. IT is only 5 Km from my home  so I can leave home only 40 minutes before flight. There is a work in progress  because of a new terminal being built  but airport functions are not interrupted by that.

Small but nice airport. A ramp would be more convenient for people carrying cabin luggage  instead of the stairs that lay down between the terminal building and the airplanes. The best airport in south eastern Europe I've been so far!
Not a lot of seating areas but clean
Miellyttävä
Small but sufficient terminal. They should open security control for domestic flights a little earlier.
Den kunne trengt et par barer til: lang kø utenfor alle! Flyplassen ser moderne ut fra utsiden  men ikke helt oppdatert inne.
Small terminal  crowded and expansive. So many stairs no escalator or easy access lift.
Nice for a regional airport
flew into Budva Tivat

It's a small airport situated on a beatiful hill. The terminal is modern and comfortable  personally i found that there were not so many shops inside  especially the Duty Free one is non very supplied

Osijek airport is a very small regional airport located approx. 20km out of the city center of Osijek. Unfortunately  there is no bus connection  only taxi for ~80HRK o/w. Security check took a while as only one lane exists.
Veldig liten flyplass med kun en bar av servering/butikker. Få destinasjoner er det også for en by med 120 000 innbyggere (to sommerruter i uken). Ingen ventende drosjer utenfor flyplassen  dårlig!
3 flights checking in at once all leaving around the same time. Airport too small to handle that many people at once at check in and through security.
Domestic departure gate is without A/C and during the summer that is not normal! Nothing to buy at departure gate as well.
For s very small airport I was surprised by how good Pula airport was  Upon arrival immigration was very smooth and bags arrived quickly. On the return trip  checkin was efficient as was security.
They didn't announce the delay of our flight in the gate displays nor there was any staff member to inform. We just sat there waiting for the plane to arrive.
As other travellers have noted  Pula is a small airport. Access from the road is first class and everything inside the airport works smoothly. Plenty of seating. One of my favourite airports.

This is a very small airport  only 4 gates. It is well organized and the atmosphere is good. Old fashioned check-in with paper boarding passes  split and the larger part collected at the gate. Passport control when we arrives  Croatia not part of Schengen agreement. Effective and did not take too long time. Overall  a good experience for a vacation week in Croatia.

Nice little airport with very friendly ground staff. Everything is located closely together and it doesn't take one very long to check in  go through the security check  etc. Unfortunately  the WiFi was terribly slow.

Small  but cool airport with great people who works there. Airport have own exit from motorway and that is good. I said  airport is small  but is nice and functional. For me  its stupid that the gates are on the floor so older people have problem there. But  its ok  you need more flights and that aiport will be more better in future! :)

Small but nice airport with own exit from motorway. Friendly stuff  easy to find everything what you need. The gates are on floor  so you must use ladder to go in plane. Like this airport  much better than airport in Rijeka.
Деревенский аэропорт.

A somehow basic terminal infrastructure with the charme of the post-communist era. Premises however are clean  well arranged and efficiently organized. Traffic peak solely during summer season  during the rest of the year  nothing doing ;-)

Very small airport. No facilities for arriving passengers  for departing passengers only a hall with a few taxfree shops. Whole airport building looks quite sombre. There is a small panorama terrace located on the roof  accessible for dep. passengers
Love the 70s style terminal  only arrived looked very basic and hasn't been updated from the feeling when walking through

It is a nice airport with nice and professional staff. Fast check in and securty. Everything was going smooth. I am always happy when i take off from Rijeka. Great car rental service at Last minute rent a car. Great vacation.I recommend to everyone.

Great great airport with beautiful nature and very friendly people. The food is excellent. Outside is a very peaceful and hear only the sounds of birds. Most beautiful island. I recommend to everyone.

Like in the Sowjet Union in the 1950/60s... Duty free closed during the season  coffee shop with one slow girl behind the bar and an open terrace to look after the planes (and to announcements about boarding or something like this)... It's not 2013!
Really bad airport. check-in and security control lasted approximately 1h even though only 40 people were travelling. NOBODY SPEAK ENGLISH THERE!
I love Split airport  as everything is always speedy  and everyone who works there is so nice towards everyone.
The split airport was good on arrival  however returning to departures. Security staff were very rude and departure lounge was very small.

The only thing I miss in this airport are boarding bridges  it would make more efficient  also they need to build a taxiway alongside the runway to improve safety and have more fast take-off and landing. Also more food kiosk needed on departing hall. No automatic check-in available

The only thing I miss in this airport are boarding bridges  it would make more efficient  also they need to build a taxiway alongside the runway to improve safety and have more fast take-off and landing.
A very nice terminal; free WiFi available in 60 min chunks; however  only one fast-food like option to eat before security.
Airport was soooo quiet. We were the only flight on the board! Felt like travelling on private jet. All good.

I took a domestic flight so my experience was some what limited. Check in and security was easy but during COVID-19 pandemic it’s is everywhere currently. Departure lounge was fine with a good view of apron and incoming flights. Good options were limited at the single shop by the domestic departure gates.
Easy access to the airport by line 37. Check-in as well as personal and border control quickly and without queues. Beautiful view from the terrace to the runway. Free and fast wi-fi.
Everything is newy short ways to walk  a viewing deck  lots of bathrooms. Also a good connection to Split with a shuttle service.

Self check-in was absuletely terrible  you can print your boarding passes  your baggage tag  but you cannot scan and send the baggage away yourself  you have to go to the normal chceck-in counter and have staff do it  obviously this means standing the queue  which renders the whole self chceck-in pointeless.

Split is relatively small  though it is being expanded- something it sorely needs as during peak season this airport gets uncomfortably crowded. The airport staff try to limit this by stopping anyone from entering the terminal more than 2 hours before the flight. The only lounge in the airport is a Croatian Airlines Business Lounge  which is accessible by Star Alliance Gold as well. This gets incredibly crowded before flights to business heavy cities with lots of flyers with status and offers some barebones snacks and a decent drink selection.
The terminal is much too small for the high season traffic. Very crowded and the AC doesn't work well enough.

This airport badly needs expansion. Currently it's one big hall with 4 gates. It's too small for the passengers it handles  that means people are everywhere  sitting on the ground  by the windows and so on. There are no restaurants (obviously because there are no space for them). Only two boots selling sandwiches and a duty free store. Also airport seems to be stuck in nineties because there is no online check in. On the bright side I heard they are building new terminal  so hopefully things will improve soon. Wifi didn't work  probably due to overcrowding also.

Nothing has changed since the 3 years we were here prior  the only thing is its now even busier... When will they invest and make this terminal what it should be... The entrance to an amazing city and the Dalmatian coast. They should be ashamed...

Oh they so need to spend some money on this old shed of a terminal. Not sure it was ever designed for major airline traffic or people  be it on the apron or the terminal facility. Best people just horrible infrastructure... Zagreb powers need to be ashamed for ignoring this...
Très très très petit terminal au vu du nombre de vol! Attendez-vous à être serrés! Néanmoins  un nouveau terminal est en construction pour rendre les voyages plus agréables.

We tried to check in online but it was impossible due to an error. We tried to contact the airport by phone but we were forwarded several times and nobody seemed to be responsible for the issue. We wanted to sit together. At the check in the unfriendly staff snurbed that we should check in online if we wanted to sit together. The security and passport check  however  was quick and easy  including friendly staff. Thanks to the check in  we had bad seat.

There was no queue at the checkin counter  its staff was very friendly. The security check was efficient  even though there was a relatively long queue it went really quick because they opened a second lane. Emigration was fast as well  the boarding process could have been more efficient by leading us directly to our plane. The wifi was free but slowly.

Wifi wasn't working. No queues at check in  security check and passport control. Boarding seemed a bit unprofessional but quickly. The staff was friendly  the toilets clean. Online check in wasn't working for several hours. Mobile boarding passes or usage of Altea DCS would be great.

This airport is too small to handle all the passengers. I flew on May 31st  and June 7th which is not really the high season  and it was packed. Not enough people working  and not enough space. The security only has 3 checking lines. There's no specific airline check in. Sometimes they make you take the bus to the airplane (walking would have taken 30 seconds).
Great airport  efficient  no waiting. Only on saturdays in the tourist season it is very busy. Splendid help checking in with a wheel chair.
it's crowdy  not user-friendly in any way. airport staff's tiny kindergarten. time has left SPU long ago.
Far too small for the number of passengers in high season.
I would say it's a good local airport. Pretty clean and in good condition. More than enough for current traffic.
Not very good  small and unequipped. Baggage reclaim is a small area leaving queues outside into the airfield. Few shops and cafes  ok boarding. Nice views however from the terminal.

Grei flyplass som ser flott ut fra utsiden med palmer og masse glass. Men  passkontrollen kan ta opp til en halvtime og terminalen kunne ha trengt en oppussing  spesielt med bagasjebåndene  der så det litt gammelt og slitt ut.
The little terminal with few places to sit. Not bad  but nothing special.
Still recovering from the Serbo Croat war.

Fin og moderne utenfra  men inne er det litt gammelt. Når man ankommer går passkøen alt for tregt! Heller ingen Duty-Free på ankomst. Men på avgang går alle køene mye fortere. Split flyplass har til og med en fin utkikksterasse.

Small airport  especially the lounge was the smallest I've ever experienced. Hardly any food or drink selection  mostly nuts  and some drinks in small glass bottles. Keep in mind there's only seats for 10 guests total.
Overall very nice airport with some AC and a lovely spotting terrace outside. Only 7 Gates but very modern and relatively clean.
Small but convenient airport  with everything you need and a outside terrace right next to the apron.
Very nice little airport. Great view of the apron from the outside terrace  always walks over the tarmac to the aircrafts  really nice for aviation lovers.

Free Wifi available and works really good when not so crowded. As more people arrive it just got really slow and almost unusable. There is only one restaurant to eat. The food was okay but price was too high for canteen food. Otherwise a nice tiny airport.

There are regular express buses from the main coach station to the airport taking about 30mins. There is a large concourse with a cafe bar outside departures which is a sun trap in the right weather and gives good views over part of the runway. The departures hall is spacious and  when I passed through  almost empty. I didn't spend too much time there as I had checked-in online. From boarding pass check to clearing security took 10mins. I felt that this was good since there was a large queue. All of the channels were open and there were several loading stations per belt  so the queue moved quickly. The queue for immigration was equally speedy. The immigration officer was friendly. Airside was quiet too. There is a small number of facilities airside  all of which had free space. I didn't notice anywhere serving proper meals. Nor did I notice any water bottle filling stations. My flight was departing from gate 17  reached by an escalator at the left hand end of the concourse. This is a small area with a simple facilities. There is probably sufficient seating for the number of passengers that a CRJ900 can carry.

There was no queue at the non-EU passport control desks and I was at the baggage reclaim very quickly. There was same delay before the baggage conveyor started. Apparently  20 minutes according to the display. I was staying close to the airport  as my original flight was due to arrive at 10pm  while this one arrived at 8pm. I had arranged to be picked up at the airport by the accommodation that I had booked. I made contact after I’d cleared customs and was picked up about 10 mins later.

Flight back was 2 hours late due to freezing fog in London. Once back at Heathrow it took 2.5 hours for the baggage to be delivered. Why it took so long to remove a couple cargo boxes from a plane and take them to baggage reclaim who knows?
Empty airport  10 min till gate  no need to hurry. No water bottle refill  no non alcoholic drinks in dutty free. Little coca colla bottle in restaurants costs like beer or wine.
As usual in Zagreb  custom check is slow  only few people working without will to live  dont mention working
On line Check-in did not send boarding card forcing us to a queue even checked already. Police border unpolite. Low quality restaurants
Aéroport très calme et propre. L'espace boutique est un peu petit. Je regrette que le tramway ou qu'un train de banlieue ne s'arrête pas à l'aéroport.
A peaceful little airport  and a very modern terminal and easy navigation outside and inside of the building. Odlično!
It is a great  modern small airport. Even very short transit times (30 min) are easily doable here. And for longer stays Wifi  several stores and food options are available.
Airport after small kid sicknesses on very beginning  now become modern and user friendly. No complaint at all.

Waiting at Zagreb airport between flights is nightmare. You don’t have properly place to wait at departure terminal  just two cafeterias  with chairs  no any space to rest. Also cafeterias are so expensive  for not so good service. Big dislike!
All in all  not so good. New airport with a lot of mistakes. I got lost at airport from exit bridge to exit point  there is no signs where to go !!!

The new terminal is very modern and spacious but it is probably also a little too spacious as it doesn't feel particularly warm and welcoming. Security and immigration are fairly quick and there is also a funky looking smoking cabin on the inside. The transit part is very small  so there wouldn't be much to do in case of a longer layover.
The airport is close to the city. The check-in line was not very long. The security check and passport check were quick. WiFi worked for the most part.
New terminal  four times bigger than the last one  fast  safe  without any problems  two ways how to get to the airport (with public transport)... Staff very polite... Big plus for new terminal :)

all though i was visiting the old terminal i had chance to see the new one.It is very modern and laaaarge.Mf final though on the old terminal is not very good.The old one is very slow and you need to pass through the Duty-free zone to do take a pee.There are no jet-bridges and the wifi is the only thing that kept me alive while waiting to board (atleast for 15 minuted as musch as it lasted).I couldn't purchase more wifi with my visa or mastercard.Hope the new terminal is much better.

Very small  packed and old fashioned. But when I was taking off I saw a new airport under construction so Zagreb soon will have a very modern airport. Very nice geen zone / park in front of the departure terminal.

An old airport. Very expensive parking and food and drinks. A new terminal is being built  but until IT is finished  I will have to use this one. IT is clean  but not nice. A capital of Croatia deserves nicer airport.

Worst and dirtiest airport IN WORLD.I am shock I DO NOT KNOW HOW AIRCRAFT can land andTAKE OFF FROM ZAGREB.MY recommendations to all people who want to visit cROATIA either town in Croatia as I am to better work around Croatia since the country is awful  I was also in Serbia BELGRADE I can say that ZAGREB CROATIA aND MUCH lagging behind Belgrade and SERBIA.I LOVE BELGRADE I could go there EVERYYEAR ALL people would TO SAY VISIT BELGRADE AND You will not regret.

Very friendly airport  easy to get around. Check-in fast  staff kind an helpful. Getting throug customs fast and well organized. No complains. Good restaurant on the first floor  with nice view of the apron. I would always give my vote to this airport. A new terminal is being built northeast of the old terminal  with a new apron  too. It will be finished in two years time.

What do you expect? Singapore Changi or Zurich Kloten? it is an airport from the former Yugoslav regime. the new terminal will be build very soon. opening in 2016. vote then please again. it will be one of the most beautiful small airports in the world! :)

Some airport workers are crazy. We almost got killed when a baggage-train-car suddenly crossed right in front of the bus that was driving us to the terminal building. Fortunately the driver managed to stop before we crashed.

This must be the worst airport in South-Eastern Europe. The lost my luggage  the airport security was so slow that I almost missed my flight. The airport lounge is terrible with one small bar with poor selection of food and drinks.
New terminal is needed  insufficient that incoming transfer passengers have to go to security check again and outgoing transfer passengers can walk right throug.
They should start building that new terminal. The old one might be sufficient  but quite ugly and not up to modern standards.
uporali się ze śniegiem i odmrażaniem samolotu - podczas śnieżycy...pełno śniegu  a asamolot wystartował - jestem pełen podziwu
Lufthavnen inneholder alt man trenger: en bar  duty-free butikk  lounge  bilutleie med mer. Men jeg har flere ganger opplevd forsinkelser her  det er synd.
Small  old  but clean and simple.

It's great that you are bused to the planes (not so much in North America). Everything happens at once at this tiny airport. Major flaw  customs wouldn't process us until they opening about 45 mins before flight (9 pm departure)
10$ for 1 hour of Internet access. It's better to go to Zagreb for a lunch bcuz food in terminal does not worth the money.
Effektiv flyplass. Kofferten ble ødelagt og dyktige ansatte gav oss erstatning.
Still recovering from the Serbo Croat war.

Ankomst: Passkontroll gikk litt tregt. Bagasjen min kom fort på båndet. Vi måtte være der noen timer for å vente på en buss  og vi fant noen deilige liggesenger i andre etasje. Hyppige avganger til byen med skyttelbuss. Avgang: Først fikk vi beskjed om a
A military base within the British Sovereign Base area with no civilian facilities. There is no access to the base without a pass
Terrible rental car parking markings! Had to loop once after i took the mandatory left turn at the smaller roundabout  then got in the wrong parking. Put some signs  people!
It was alright  everything there  the private transfers  but I didn't really like the staff  but it was enjoyable
A little slow on the baggage coming out  also being a Business passenger with so called priority luggage waiting 20 minutes after the first bags appeared  isn't my idea of priority.
Hi! Larnaca airport was not so good. the check in took almost an hour and the food was really expensive

Overcrowded - spent more than 2 hours standing in huge queues for check-in  security control  passport control. Too much stress. I found i it totally unavailable for elderly or passengers with minor (but still) problems to stand for so much time.
Horribe Toilets  horriblen and confusing gate. You really don´t know which gate. wifi is also weak. But at least shops and security check is nice.

Incompetent ground staff that are not trained in Airlines Pre-check systems. Result- slow boarding progress. The security staff is far below industry standard and does not fulfill IATA standards. Luggage is not checked thoroughly.
Very poor service at check-in  almost appeared as though they weren't sure of what they were doing. No fast track for Business passengers and no priority boarding.
Very poor service at check-in  spent 20 minutes talking to each other rather than starting the process. No fast track for Business class passengers.

A small but very clean  nicely laid out and Hassle free airport. Check in efficient as was security. A larger than usual duty free area  plenty to relieve you of those surplus euros! The options for dining are quite limited but in keeping for an airport of this size i’d say. No long walk to the gates which all appeared to be serviced by air bridges  so any prevailing weather rendered irrelevant. My arrival here also went without incident so  taxis right outside  nice and handy. Overall a very satisfactory experience.

LCA is a fantastic airport with hourly bus rides to the main cities on the island. This makes it extremely accessible from all parts of the country. There are many food options at the airport and terminal building is very nice. Quite spacious  clean and with lots of natural light. One of the best airports around.
Too long! Spent a lot of time in lines! About 2 hours from enter to the airport till security finish! It is terrible!

immigration machines don't make any sense - if they are there to speed up the process  they don't work. You spend your time at the counter and then  waiting in a line to an officer  longer than relgular immigration process
Too many passangers with too less food services. The Wifi authorization is complicated: you need internet connection to finish the authorization process...
Got there 1 hour before depart  more than enough. Baggage control doesn't really care about liquids: They don't ask you to take the liquids bag out.
The new terminal is really making the difference. Easy to reach  good check-in facilities  gate area very nice.
check-in computer systems failed within the airport  which created a huge chaos and delays at departures

Seems like the airport cleaning team can not cope with the growing number of passengers at the airport. One thing I always loved about LCA is that it was clean. This time that was not the case. The gate area was filthy with crumbs and chips all over the floor while the windows were very 'oily.' Very disappointed.
Wifi didn't work  very expensive duty free  Nice that it were two tunnels to our craft to the begin door and to the middle
One of the best airports I've ever seen. Very clean and friendly. The staff is helpful and polite  very professional.

The airport in Larnaca is very good and comfortable. The only thing that didn't work out was that I couldn't find any wi-fi. But all the rest was fine. Ah  and I was very surprised that on security check they have someone who picks your things and organizes them. It's such a good thing!
this airport in Cyprus is good its easy to get to the airport their are lots of facilities like shops and restaurants the immigration queues are terrible [1.30hours]
Good airport with good facilities. The security check is going smoothly and the personnel is very kind and helpful.

Public transport in Cyprus is underdeveloped  but the airport is easy to get to (and from) from all major cities not that the Travel Express shared minibus service is licensed to use the airport. The terminal facilities are as good as can be expected from an airport in a small country and immigration/customs are normally fast and pain free. Security is good as well. Queues are normally kept down and it's surprisingly efficient. The lounge has a small selection of food and looks past its best.

Very cool airport. Quick Check and plenty of space for waiting flight. Lots of shops and bars. Great air conditioning. Excellent transport to the airport. Airport services speaks perfect English. Only negative is badly running wifi!
Not a big airport but very spacious and open in the inside. Couldn't connect to wifi on my phone for some reason. Was not busy so customs and security was quick. Lots of different choices for food.
very nice airport but a bit disorientating. lovely staff spoke perfect english and made the airport feel like home! runway was a bit rough but that may have been wind

My favourite airport to fly to without a doubt. Always delivers a great sevice. Passport control always quick. Baggage is always out quickly. Singage is great. There are plenty of toilets on arrival with a duty free shop and bank ect. On departure the check in is brilliant easy to find you're desk cue's are minimal. Security again is very quick its never taken me more than 5 mins to get through it. In the departure lounge there are plenty of facilities  a large duty free shop  a bar  a buger king and plenty of toilets. There is also plenty of seating while you wait for you're flight. Being a modern airport the signage is very good and all gates are easy to find.

Larnaca airport is truly fantastic. The terminal is modern with a lot of light and a wide selection of shops. The Kapnos shuttle service tends to be unreliable  especially if you are catching one of those 05.30 departures.

The airport's new terminal building is great and clean. However if you don't arrive in time (at least 2 hours prior departure) it might be quite challenging not to miss your flight as passengers are queueing in a huge line to get through the security and/or passport check (I'm talking about our experience as EU citizens in October... I can only imagine the situation during the high season...). The airport's smoking area within the terminal building is a huge plus though.. loved it!

I have never experienced such a slow check in. 1 hour in line to check in 200 persons  with 6 counters open. Security was ok  but also took its time as well as passport conbtrol. On this airport you should be at leaset 2 1/2 hour before departure  at least if you have luggage to check in.

Nice small airport with exceptionally low prices. And even if the airport itself is quite small  there are numerous shopping facilities  restaurants  dutyfree and all. Easy to navigate  smooth check-in and security. Free WiFi.
Я там забыла пиджак! Не вернули... =)
Spacious and modern. Little hot though.
nice airport!
Just nice airport. Everything is fast.
Good modern facilities  could have more shops and lower prices.
Great airport  probably one of the better ones in Europe. Clean  spacious with a lot of natural light and most importantly efficient.
Nice  new airport  very easy to get to the gates.
Spacious  clean  fast and nice airport.
wiadomość o poóźnieniu i nie zrobili z tym nic  wiedzieli  że mam czas na przesiadkę tylko 30 min  opóźnienie nawet do godziny (plan wylot 16:25  new time: 17:00)
Do not buy anything at the Larnaca duty free on the way out. They do not know the regulations at other airports that you may pass through. We had our purchases confiscated at Bangkok.
very nice new airport
Nice and new airport  shopping area not very big  but is probably the fastest airport i've been. you can arrive 1 hour before the flight  and u gonna wait 24 minutes in front of the gate:)
Nice small airport !
Eating areas very warm  good shopping though. Waiting area seats a bit hard. Clean toilets
Big airport - great atmosphere
Holding area too small. Checkin chaos. Toilets bad. FIDS archaic.
nice airport
Pretty poor and very crowed. Bus stand.
Very good new airport
A good and new airport. Fairly quick and efficient.
nice hot weather  sunny day  nice new airport and perfekt holiday location

Small airport  very easy to get through. Queues at customs on arrival were being managed by staff even after midnight. Security staff were friendly. Toilets were clean on both occasions. Was travelling on the first flight of the day at 05:45 but the shops and food outlets in the departure lounge were already open. A pleasure to travel to and from.
Small airport but so quick and easy to get through  staff nice and not stressing like some countries. Smooth path through customs ????
Very good experience today  efficient and friendly service throughout. A quiet morning but all facilities were available

Airport is VERY expensive as far as airports go. Expect to spend EURO15/20 per person for a very basic meal with a soft drink. The check in and security process is probably the most efficient I've ever but the rest of it lacks in staff proactivity.

Basic airport with basic facilities. The food outlets airside we saw were rubbish and as usual  a rip-off. Taxis are also a rip off. Always refuse the first price as they negotiate. I actually walked away and the driver came after me to offer a lower price.
Baggage claim took really long and they didn't even bring the buss we were supposed to go on so we had to walk in the boiling hot weather the toilets were clean enough and staff were nice

Departing Pafos is difficult due to Cypriot Covid restrictions. Check in and immigration are OK. What let's it down is the departure lounge. Every other seat not in use  so departing at weekends is hit or miss whether you get a seat or not. Announcements may as well be al in Greek (they are in English 50/50). Impossible to hear and actually a waste of effort.
Arriving in Pafos is OK now that Covid testing has stopped. Still needed my Cyprus travel pass and Vaccination certificate. Baggage claim is hit and miss.
Despite Covid they have made people to crowed within very small area with no chance to keep the right disctance.
I had no problems with this great airport It such a nice airport nothing bad i have to say about it!
Brilliant Service Quick through security. Great food. Can recommend central bar. Our flight was so well boarded.

Loved this airport  very modern for an island airport  check in and security was quick. Great selection of shops and restaurants  prices were as usual expensive. Departures area was very big with lots of seating.

Nice airport  close to the city of Paphos ando to the highway to Limassol. The security check has been very fast and the duty free shop is quite big. Despite this I found a bug under one of the seats in the waiting area  Cleaning improvement needed.
Another airport that feels justified in charging criminal prices for soft drinks after security and has the nerve to ask for boarding passes to buy them.
Arrival at this small airport is a breeze. Customs is often understaffed but baggage claim is quick and efficient. Excellent car pick-up service.

Small airport that gets you on your plane rather quickly. Walk-up/walk-off set up  better than having to crowd onto a bus to get to the plane. Food is overpriced. Get yourself a coffee before going through security as choices are not as good once inside. Shops are okay. The lounge is basic and straightforward. The best part is the car drop-off/pick-up. Very convenient!

Chaotic! Two flights trying to board through the same gate. Information screen kept changing details but still getting it wrong. Cabin crews had keep sorting out passengers! Lounge jam packed ~ heaven only knows what it would be like inthe middle of summer. Lounge overflowed into 'cattle sheds' outside  then when they were full onto the aircraft standing on the tarmac.

The smaller of Cyprus' airports. Very easy to get to from Pafos by bus  and from the rest of the government controlled cities by Travel Express. Facilities are better than you'd expect from such a small airport. The only downside is you can expect to be waiting in the heat on the tarmac if you're flying a budget airline.

Paphos airport was very good but there was a lack of facilities (I.e. Only duty free and 1 or 2 other shops. Also there some flights that weren't announced. All the staff were very friendly. I didn't think the security was as safe as the UK and you need to show your passport once you have gone through the security check point. The food court has reasonable food at good prices. Paphos airport is more of a holiday airport.

Fairly average airport  has a good amount of shops and food services too. Baggage claim was really quick  probably because we arrived at a late time so no one else except people from our flight were there. Took a long time finding the airport when coming back because there was a lot of road works going on around the area. There is also  as far as I could tell  only one road into the airport. Immigration/customs took less than 20 minutes. Overall a more than average airport. I didn't check  but I'm not sure whether there is any free WiFi  which is kind of annoying.
