I think I preferred the old Paphos terminal with the outside seating. This is just a bog standard EasyRyan pile them on terminal with minimal facilities and duty free more expensive than local retail.

I`ve got only carry-on baggage so I should not rate Baggage claim. Getting to the airport is very simple. From Harbour station (main station for the bus in Pafos center) bus 612 is going directly to the airport. Cost 1 5 euro (for students 0 75 euro). Airport is small and good marked so there is no problem with find proper gate. If you have a lot of time you can go to the seashore. It is 5 minutes walk from airport.
Aircraft parked only

A very nice  well designed modern airport. All departures in one building  all gates in one area. All gates connected to a bus which takes you to your plane. Good selection of well known shops  good prices  friendly security staff.

Not well connected to the highway. The terminal itself is a bit boring but small and accessible so there is no need to arrive 2 hours before a flight. Costa Coffee is available outside of security. The duty free shops are adequate. Walk to aircraft.
benches without armrests - quite good place to sleep
Very Basic  2 shops and a bar.
Big queues to check in and appeared disorganised. Signage unclear. Not enough seating in departure lounge

Bag drop was terrible. Waited 40 minutes  check in woman was miserable as sin. Did not speak at all. Security very rude and trying to rush everyone through telling all to "Hurry Up" treated like animals.
not that good food
Kleiner Airport  sauber  effizient und freundlich mit gutem Duty-Free-Shop für Whiskyfans
Very good and quick check in
Very crowded check-in area and departure lounge.
Small airport. Basic but efficient.

It is not an airport really  it looks like a small Greyhound station from the 60’s but without the amenities ... even back then (!) It’s just a large extremely dirty loud room with 2 counters to check in... and 200 packed passengers shouting. A few military guards to check your ID and flight ticket  no metallic portal  no computer  no seat  no toilet. I went once only to pick up someone. I was astonished. I flew to other airports in third-world countries  this is the worst I’ve seen so far. It is also extremely dangerous if you are not familiar with the area - in times of war as well as peace. Update: just saw travelers’ pictures  the airport now has seats and sells drinks (2020)
The new facilities for immigration and luggage are impressive  and quiet for those who new the old ones - even if my luggage took 45' to appear today

The new airport facilities are great  as well on arrival as on departure. Although on departure there are still too many checkpoints. Also  once on the tarmac  under the burning sun  we all had to queue again for 3 other checks before boarding the plane. P.S. I don't see the need of checking the international vaccination card on exciting.
Departure from Ndjili is a headache !! Very bad organisation of check-in and departure formalities !
Stop in Douala

I have been to several African airports but have never seen an airport where everything is such a mess  from bizarre formalities to get your passport back to the check-in process which is completely surrealistic
Very nice airport with free parking. Friendly and helpful staff. One of the best lounge I ever have been at

Aalborg airport is a nice airport to fly from. free parking  easy to find your way around the airport  easy check in and happy and pleasant staff. following flight airlines fly from Aalborg Airport. KLM  Lufthansa  norwegian  SAS  Jettime  Thomas cook  Vueling plus many more. Good trip...

Definitely my favourite airport by a huge margin. Easy to locate  access and no queues at all. Staff is friendly and very helpful. Only one major "tax-free" shops as opposed to CPH or LGW with multiple shops.

Relatively small  and not that busy  but a few flights now and then. Very clean and effective. My favourite airport by far! Very fast security and great selection of foods  drinks and goods from the couple of shops.

Brilliant regional airport  free parking  easy to come to and from  close to the city. Refurbished resently. can only recommend. Nice duty free  friendly staff  easily accessible. Check-in is easy too as there are many machines for self check-in.

Getting to the airport is easy. As the airport is only a small one  there are no expensive airport busses - all busses serving the airport are just regular busses. Check-in is easy too as there are many machines for self check-in. Please be aware of the fact that it can take some time checking in baggage. Security check are good with an average waiting time of 0 to 5 minutes depending on when you're travelling. Terminal facilities could be better  althrough they are really good as they are now. Food are just as expensive as in any other normal airport.

Brilliant regional airport  all just refurbished. Nice duty free  friendly staff  easily accessible. Abundance of free parking just outside terminal. Growing in terms of number of destinations. Probably the best regional airport in all of Denmark.
Newly renovated airport not far from home. It's not a big airport  which definantely is a plus.
Nice  airy  good areas. Children's room and check in & security superb.
My second home. I fly from here every second week! :-)
Fantastic airport. Love that you can walk from your parking and under two minutes later you're on the other side of security.

Aarhus Airport is a wonderful airport  it's always a pleasure to fly from there. There's two things I wish in the future; 1. Letbane (light rail) from Aarhus to the Airport. 2. Motorway all the way to Grenaa
No doubt that the big renovation Aarhus Airport have made is a game changer. It's good to see more companies join the airport.
AAR has undergone a massive upgrade. Lovely airport to travel from now. The terminal is basically one big lounge.
Brilliant airport where everyone can get a first class experience. Great lounge  great furnitures  great food and a lovely playroom for children.

Aarhus Airport is the best airport I know  not because they have loads of shops that I like or anything like that. They only have two  one before security and one after. But I still yet love this regional airport  it's once again the best airport I've ever known. Customs were quick  mainly because there weren't any. The baggage arrived fast aswell.

Aarhus Airport is the best airport I know  not because they have loads of shops that I like or anything like that. They only have two  one before security and one after. But I still yet love this regional airport  it's once again the best airport I've ever known. The baggage claim is always quick  same thing with customs (Mainly because the customs aren't really being used). Overall  5/5 star airport.

Very small airport. Seems odd when it serves the 2nd largest city in Denmark. Only way to get to the airport is by car  taxi or bus from Aarhus. Don't expect extravaganza terminal boutiques or food places. I think they have 15 flight  pr. day! Friendly staff though

Well..... A small local airport  in the middle of nowhere. That's basically what this is. A single carrousel  a single mens room and a single womens room and a immigration officer. That's what you see when you enter the arrivals. Very Scandinavian 70's tiles on the floor - yes  the brown ones  and well just far from anything really. Not like Stansted or Aalborg at all!
Nice local airport  but with very small taxfree shopping and you have to walk out to the airplane (Short walk)
Lovely  tiny airport which has everything you need. You can grab something to eat  shop for snacks and souvenirs  all without the crowd of a big airport. And there's a LEGO store!
Small  fast and good restaurants. You have everything needed. But very shitty location and public transport.

Very bad airport location. It takes forever to come back home. If you land after 23:00  forget about the bus and pay fortunes in taxi because te airport is located in the middle of nowhere. 160€ of taxi to reach Esbjerg (70 km)  240€ of taxi to Reach Aarhus (100 km). Airlines should have a look at maps before serving a 11 000 people town.
Ground access time is too long. The airport is badly situated. Airlines should serve Aarhus and Esbjerg instead of this airport.

Worst location ever for an airport  fare from any city. I would prefer airlines to serve Aarhus or Esbjerg  rather than this ultra low cost location. It looks like reaching Beauvais or Bergamo  but with very expensive tickets. This is not a good experience.

The airport is too far from any big city. I am waisting my time just to reach this location in the middle of nowhere. Airlines should better serve Aarhus and Esbjerg instead of this ultra low-cost location.

This small airport is a short bus ride from town for a reasonable price. The airport was quiet for the first few flights of the year. Security only took 5 minutes  even through my backpack had to go through twice. My camera was the problem. I long for the day when security rules are the same at every airport. There are few facilities airside. There are very few toilets and only a bakery for food and drink. There wasn't much seating that I could see. I departed from the far right of the terminal and once through the barrier there was a small seating area with tables and chairs. Large enough for the small aircraft that I was departing on. However  the barrier is at the bottom of a stair and there is little space while we were held there for about 10 minutes.
Small quiet airport but clean  modern and efficient. If you want to get a train to anywhere else in Denmark  you need the bus to Vejle as there is no train station in Billund ????

First airport where you can’t get your baby stroller to the plane door. Also they start the embarking but the plane is not ready  we stayed just in cold in the airbridge  but people that boarded trough back door of the plane also needed to wait in rain.
One of the smaller airports but very nice  clean and effective airport. Very fast security check and nice personal. Easy orientation through the whole airport. Easy access to the gates.

You can get to the airport by bus  car and taxi. Unfortunately  public transport to the airport is very limited. The airport is small but handles the passengers and aircraft really well. I am unsure of WiFi as I live in Denmark so I just use my own data. For the size of the airport there is plenty of places to eat. Security lines move really fast and the check-in is smooth.
PLease avoid this place. Worn and decrepit. Security is staffed by the most rude people they can find. Lounge is joke - practically no food. no cups: "just go get them in the kitchen"! - really!

For all Lego fans out here; there is a great Lego-shop behind the security-line. For all departing from Billund  visit that awesome shop with great discounts on Lego-sets! And there are table football in the right corner of the waiting lounge.
Small  boring. Not too much options to spend money. If your flight gets cancelled you will experience hell if you're at the end of the line - i was waiting 5 hours in line to get my ticket rebooked.

BLL knew that they had an aircraft change  but didnt bother to tell the passengers...this caused a lot of confusion onboard.. Also when at ETD 0945 all pax still at gate. bording started at 1010  ith out any info about why we had a delay..Once off block at 1050 we still waited about 30 minutes before take off

Generally a very modern and nice airport. Many shops in waiting area. Many flights leaving at the same time in the early morning makes long ques in security check. Waiting and process times up to around 30 mins.

As per usual  Billund is a fantastic airport. It's easy to get to  and you're not able to miss it. Free Wifi - works everywhere on the premises. Check-in  security and boarding without a hassle. The big Taxfree shop provides basically all you need for your holidays or the trip  and everything is clearly marked for inside or outside the EU.

Restaurants close before the late flight has departed  even more annoying as we couldn't get any food during our 30 min delay. The only open eatery had sandwiches and pizzas that looked as if they had been there since the middle ages. Not quite good enough. Cheap tax free prices though.
Excellent regional airport  very efficient arrival process.
Very good airports for newbies
Very nice airport. Not much more to say.
Possibly my favourite airport in Europe. Billund is smart and well laid out  the only issue is that the waiting areas at the gates are a little small for a 189-seat Ryanair 737!
They have too much airspace for such a small airport.
Top Class Airport :-D
Has what you need.
Open - friendly - very good.
overskuelig størrelse lufthavn
Nice airport whit good service
Terminal Lounge only permits use if you are flying with the relevant airline or have a Priority Pass
Quite convenient except a bad choice of location for the charging port at the gate  as the brick for the charger wouldn't stay as it hit a slab under it.
180 DKK (around 25-30 USD) is a bit too expensive for a sandwitch. A hard pass for anyone wishing to have an airport meal.

One of the worst airports in Europe by the facilities and security check. Public transport is good tho. Very slow security check  old machines  you have to take out everything from your bag  they frequently send your baggage through again. The food facility selection is the worst  and they close around 9-10 pm. There is nothing to do there. Very bad. All lounge close very early.

I think that the only people in the immigration queue were from the same flight as me  and since I was near the front of the aircraft  I was through in a few minutes. My case was already on the carousel when I got there and after buying my ticket; had only a few minutes to wait for a train. Very speedy and hassle free.
Alot of ways to get to the airport via train. Chech-in line quite short and it went fast. Security quick and alot of stores and resturants. did*t try the WiFi

The check-in process was slow and quite cramped--it seems the airport is running out of available space for check-in counters. Otherwise  emigration and security was very efficient and there were plenty of food options in the gate areas.

The terminal seems to be showing its age  as the arrivals corridor was old and cramped  and the bathrooms were very small and quite dirty. Immigration and the baggage claim process was smooth  and finding the metro was very easy.

Getting to the airport was nice as it was cold and a bit busy  not much services but there is a self-service starbucks machine which is quite cool. WiFi was actually quite good. No immigration as its a regional flight. Baggage claim was not so great as the room is small and busy. Overall great airport for a city like Copenhagen.

Just fine. We stood at the check-in for two hours (BEFORE security) because our ticket wasn't available. Not going to be flying BA/AA Codeshare anymore. Absoulutely crazy. The check-in area was very dimly lit  making it hard for the seniors in our group over 85 years old  to see. Security was fine minus some jackass arguing over a 24 OUNCE bottle of moisturizer. Please learn and follow the rules  you will not win  especially against a danish security officer!! Very well handled. So much for zone boarding. Zone was called and they didn't enforce the boarding process at all. I was in business class to LHR and people just stuck their giant roller bags up front I before walking all the way back to row 328  so I had nowhere to store my bag. Oh well  not trying to whine or anything but it is annoying getting run over helping your grandmother who needs extra time and space by some impatient tit. Staff was friendly though. We were delayed seemingly endlessly because a tug from 3 gates down couldn't push an aircraft back? I am an aviation management and flight technology major and that made no sense. It is what it is.
I live near this airport really good! Alot of food places and toilets around the airport i give this a 4 star rating!

Easy access to the airport by car  train and metro  it's very near (12km) Copenhagen city center. Check-in and security is fast. On a good day you can check in  get through security and be airside in 25-30 min.  for international flights where self service is not available it will be the standard 1hr-1.5hr unless you are first in line at check-in. There are lots of restaurants airside  fresh sandwich bars  hotdog stands  a steak house  gourmet pizza and sushi options. Shopping is also fine  ranging from Lego to Gucci stores. I can recommend Carls "smørebrød" and a large microbrew pint to start your trip.
The airport is nice  but the passport check for F gates was closed before our flight was boarding 2 hours late  so we had to do a large detour back to C gates and then back to the F gates

Getting to the airport was easy with public transport. Check in was easy. Security check was quick but they were a bit serious. The checked my luggage for drugs. Terminal was too big. Long walks. Wifi was fast enough. A lot of food options.
long walk from gate to the exit. public transport facilities in the late evening was great. everything was closed.

Arrived to terminal 1 hour late from AMS. No sign or info on what what bagagebelt. Had to search several belts and lost another hour. No personnel in place. CPH in the evening early night is a dead place. The danes have a serious problem in manning the different positions. Plane was 1 hour late due to lack of ATC-personell. And then an extra hour to find luggage. Avoid Copenhagen Airport if You can! The service is much better in small local airports around Europe.

Copenhagen Airport is very straight forward to reach  you can get there in a lot of different ways. The security check however is over queued and there is a big lack of staff  but the rest of the airport was good.
Yummy cinnamon rolls and local apple/pear juice :) Easy to fine a way anywhere and ground staff is helpful when needed :D

Be aware  at the security lines they are very strict about liquids. It needs to fit in a tiny 1 liter bag. Even if your liquids do not exceed 1 liter  it has to fit in the tiny bag (1 per person)  otherwise you have to throw it away. The rest of the airport is really nice and has a great vibe. Recommend checking out the Mikkeler bar at terminal 2!
Your typical bigger airport  nothing special for me. You can find there everything you need. Besides that  the DSB-STAFF was pretty friendly and helpful.

Don't go out on the new pier with planes to the US if traveling from other EU countries  the pier is brand new but no cafes  shops or even vending machines exist on the far side of the passport control.

Excellent airport with great services  interesting retail options beyond the usual  bog-standard international brands that you see everywhere else  efficient layout. Some of the public areas are slowly getting into a state of mild disrepair that could easily be fixed - let's hope they get to it.
Very easy to navigate with good signage. Baggage retrieval was prompt. Great transportation in and out of the airport.
30 minute waiting time upon security check and huge queue. Also the lounge was so full that there was no space available
There is an issue with lack of staff for security which has resulted in a lot of long queues and delays. Beware until this is resolved and be there earlier.

Getting to the airport is simple. There are regular services from the main train station in the city centre with a journey time of around 15 minutes. I had check-in on line with no cabin babs  so I headed straight to security. Security was relatively efficient and I was through in a record time for me. There was a bit of a wait at passport control in the non-EU queue. I found that the toilets were well hidden in the terminal building. I also felt that the terminal was a bit sprawling. all of the gates seem to be so far from the exit from the duty free shop. The gate that I departed from had few seats  but they started "boarding" just as I arrived.

I passed through the airport with little difficulty. I was in row 1 with only a cabin bag. There was a slight queue at passport control  but it didn't take me more than 5 minutes to clear. I travelled to the city by train and there are several ticket machines available to purchase tickets. The trains are regular and the trip takes only 15 mins. The airport station has two platforms. make sure that you choose the correct one  otherwise you will end up in Sweden.
The flight left gate E129. Was like a desert. 10 min walk from C-gates in Term 3. only 3 vendingmachines with softdrinks and sweets and 1 with coffee.

When boarding started  all passengers with transit flights had to wait for almost 1 hour! The personell at the gate was unwilling to help the one guy that had to do everything by himself regarding checking covid19 documentation. Finally after the plane was allready 30min delayed  due to the incompetent staff  2 addidtional people came to help. They should start boarding 1hour earlier if they cant get better at it.

SAS Lounge CPH is worse than a public toilet at the autobahn. Filthy  noisy  children running and screaming  gents restroom smelling of urine  sticky floors  tables never cleaned up. SAS is loosing money apparently...
Good airport  nice and close to the city center. Food is very expensive here. I paid 130 kronen (almost 20€) for 2 buns with ham and cheese  so try to bring some food from somewhere else if possible
As always at Kastrup  the prices are out of this world - DKK70 for a cup of coffee and an old croissant at Espresso House - extorsion.
Non-Schengen to Schengen connections are seamless here–even though we just had about an hour  we had no problems at all making our connecting flight.
Long bus ride from the aircraft to the terminal. Also kilometres to walk to the baggage claim area and finally we still had to wait on the luggage. This can be improved.

Nice and fast security! Security officers are polite and professionals! Bilingual and they smile! Duty free is huge and vast variety! Elegance is the key for the stores and halls! Great airport great lounges!
Early days of Corona. Most places closed. Lots of announcements to social distance. Travelling business but lounge closed.
Absolutely brilliant airport. Extremely friendly staff  easy to follow signage and very nice atmosphere. Great views over ramp  good food and shop choice  and free high speed WiFi was a bonus.
Almost the last gate at the far away from the entrance. Tho get there it took 35 minutes at least with bad knees

It was a very nice airport  getting to airport was easy butin the check-in line there was so much people... Security check was very very fast and great! Shops and WiFi was great but lounge was not very clean..

The check-in area for the flight was in terminal 2 which was a little bit far from the train stop. It took about half an hour to the airport from the city central by train. However  the instruction for the train at Central Train Station was not clearly shown. The self-check-in kiosks printed all the boarding passes for perspective flight (BA819  BA27). However  it didn’t print the baggage tags. It happened as same as the other kiosks in that area. As the result  I needed to go to the counter for further assistance. The staff was pleasant to proceed the bag drop procedure. The indication of security check was clearly revealed. There was a Lego figure which represented the symbol of Denmark in the hall. The security check provided a distribution machine for dividing passengers to various aisles. It typically reduced the time for waiting. The tax refund counter was difficult to find  even many staffs can’t tell the exact location. Nevertheless  the airport contained lots of notable stores in restricted area  which enriched the entertainment and shopping features. The immigration section was placed at the middle of the key corridor of the gates. Although both C10 and C27 were gates C  C10 was proceeded as domestic flight which was ahead of the migration  instead C27 was following by an immigration barrier.

Great transport opportunities to and from the airport with the same fare for any transport means. Terminals looks nice and bright and the airport is still expanding which I will look forward to see the result of. Lots of dining/snack opportunities but in the expensive end.
We went to the airport with the train. And we have fast track and SAS lounge. So from the train to the lounge took around 10min.

A very nice airport with great facilities. Extremely easy access by metro in only 15 minutes from center of Copenhagen. Has an easy layout and you can cross the entire airport for transfers in about 20 minutes  expect for a few outlying gates. A wide variety of food and shops. Check-in is easy and completely digital for most airlines. Security has been expanded and improved significantly over the last years and is getting better and better  but some automation would still help. Passport control has a few automated booths  but they are not working so great yet. Baggage claim is easy and effective.
The digital maps at CPH weren’t working and the gate number shows up quite late  but a very great airport

We were at nearby gate C30 some of passengers seated on the floor  others just standing up  very few seats. Pre-boarding check only get you the chance to get a decent seat. Very bad experience  for me not much problem  but for people with bones problems is very difficult waiting so many time standing up or lay on the floor. Boarding process very was very messy also.
In base it is a fine airport  there are many restaurants and shops. The toilets however are pretty bad... for that 3 star  not 4

Very expensive. Not enough seats for all the travelers. There are only a few. More seats only at the store where you have to buy something to seat. There are only a few charging ports for the mobile between shops and in front of toilet's  so you have to sit on the ground. Really bad in eyes.
Friendliest people in security  immigration  great selection for shops and food. Most people working at the airport were very professional. Easy connection from the city via the train.

As most of the time the security check (SC) stinks. Unfriendly  bossy etc. Some of the friendliest SC personal I have experienced was this year at IAD and SYR  just within the last four weeks and two days ago.
Very poor Immigration and Baggage claim. It took us 2 hours to get trough the airport  and we nearly missed our train.
Getting to the airport was easy due to the metro. Check-in was a bit chaotic but that is mainly due to SAS self service. The terminal was very clean and there was plenty to do.

Kopenhagen Airport is a nice and quick airport. When flying with Lufthansa you can fast track to check in your bagage  security is nice and swift  a lot better than other airports. Wifi is pretty fast  altough there aren't that many big windows to take pictures out of. Did not visit the lounge.
Desk only opens 2 hours before flight  no bag drop option for those of us who have already checked in. Delayed by non checked in passengers and had no time to relax and/or shop at the airport.

small airport and you cant do much if you dont have a visa to leave during a layover. barely few shops post immigration and the lounge is very expensive and only available for a limited time. try to avoid copenhagen for long layovers. you can see planes from mostly everywhere

One of my favorite airports in the EU to change planes  convenient connections  easy to get around  not too big or too confusing like Frankfurt. Sadly  the immigration service didn't bother to open the eGates. They did so after I got through (around 07h15)   while there were already massive lines waiting to cross the border and get to their connections.

The main parts of the terminals are quite good  charging points  lounges  cafés  variety of restaurants are there. But the F gates...terrible: limited chairs  one shop  and is 20 minutes walk (through some big buildings  which are under construction) from the center of the terminal.
In general a very expensive airport. If you don't / can't use lounges  there's not much places just to sit and wait for your flight without paying for something (drinks or foods)
Very pleasant airport  nice SAS lounge. Size-wise perfect  easy to find your way  wooden floors give it a nice atmosphere
Fantastic airport with excellent and friendly staff from check in staff  though to security  customs and immigration. Very clean airport and open. One of the best I've ever been after Changi SIN.
Only transit but as our flight was delayed  the connecting flight was parked close to our gate so we could quickly change aircraft.

I only made a quick transfer at the airport. As the flight was delayed the airport made it possible for the aircraft to park close to our connecting flight so that's why the overall score is high although I didn't spend much time there.
Amazing airport  every possible way of getting to the airport  bus  train  coaches  taxi. Many facilities including viewing platforms.

Long distance between gates which may be bothersome for some  but not me. Fairly great view of the planes taking off or landning  but the distance between the terminal and the runway is too far for it to be entertaining. Good selection of restaurants and shops make it possible to kill a maximum of 4 hours in connection time.

They have managed to lenghten again the distance from the main terminal to the F-gates  by making the temporary passage still more sinous (May 2018) - those works in progress do progress very slowly !

Barely any seats at all to sit on whilst waiting for your flight at the gate. There were 24 seats in total for all the passengers waiting for their A320 to arrive. The airport has a nice runway layout though for departures to the south since the end of rwy 22R is south of the terminals  and therefore barely any taxiing at all.
Always good experience on airport. I forgot my ID but they made a personal call and I got it back in minutes.

Gigantic airport with great facilities but our gate F4 was really really far out! Nothing nearby at all  felt like going to the wrong direction. Felt like there are not enough seats that are not any restaurant's seats.

(Lufthansa  Terminal C) Copenhagens Terminal 3 is a clean and well signposted terminal. At the moment huge parts are under maintenance/modification so the terminal can not be used in its full size. The WiFI was apparently not working that evening so I am not able to say something about the surf speed. From arriving at CPH to departing CPH by train it took me around 10 minutes (I was just traveling with hand baggage)
Excellent connectivity to metro & regional train. Nice airport but a bit discombobulated with construction & it’s a bit small for the amount of people that were there. Could use more restaurants.

Loved this airport  easy access via automated rail from the city every 10-20 min. Check in was easy  security easy with priority  and LOTs of shopping and huge duty free. Check out the bakery by the entry to International gates  amazing.
Copenhagen is an amazing airport  has everything  efficint and fast. Never had a problem. 10/10 would fly here again
Worse baggage claim in the world even has LCD screens telling you need to wait every time for 30 mins. After already walking some distance and time.

This is quite a big airport and involved a bit of a walk to the train station  there are two so make sure you go to the last one to the left if you want to get to the city centreat the north side. Also there are no ticket offices  just a machine that requires coins or a card. I used my visa as it does not take notes. So from landing to the city centre took me about 45 minutes. Not bad at all.
So big. We had to walk from the door to remove the luggage  I calculate that two or three kilometers

Copenhagen Kastrup is the airport I've connected most flights in  and I don't enjoy it that much. Usually it's between a domestic danish flight and an international flight to Stockholm or the opposite way around. When one of the planes get delayed  I usually have to run to be able to get to the other plane on time. It feels a bit as a shopping mall when trying to connect  cause the only thing between the two diffrent sections of the airport is shops  annoying shops. Now I shouldn't blame the shops for existing  when I have a longer connection time their nice to look in. WiFi is free  which is very nice. Food and retail services are completely fine  but as I described before I sometimes hate them.

Copenhagen Airport is generally a very good airport  easy to get around  good facilities  clean  and professional. Even though the airport advertises with Wi-Fi and the Wi-Fi is selectable  in practice it dosn't work (haven't done for over a year now). If at all you are able to connect to the Wi-Fi  there is no connection to the Internet.
Fantastic disability assistance on transfer. Wheelchairs and mobile elevators waiting and very pleasant and helpful staff. Wonderful.
Flying SAS. Bad gate seating space and dirty toilets at gate D4. Great food options. Could not get wifi to work.
Had a short early morning layover so I didn't get to see much of the airport  but from what i saw it was great. Loads of shops and plenty of places to eat.

This airport is well known for good shopping and dining high to airport prices But the standard of cleaning is horrible. It is clear for everyone that only the absolutely neccesary cleaning is standard for this airport manangement. But it is NOT enough.

Not many places to sit. Gates at weird places. Very few flight information counters that are difficult to see because of being so high up. And just one place to buy postcards - really? I just didn't like the feel of this airport. The wifi is ok once you find out how to turn it on.

Only one boarder post was open to deal with the arrivals from our flight  which was a bit of a pain  queuing about 20mins to get through there. Departures went a lot better  security was fast  and rest of the airport experience was very favourable.
Wonderful Airport  very clean and pleasant.Staff extremely polite and just great airport to travel to.

It's big and very busy airport. There was a big waiting line for security check  but the staff was very nice. They would hand you plastic bags for your liquids if you don't have one. The terminal facilities were great. Overall great experience.

It's big and very busy airport. The wi-fi connection was very slow and hard to connect to. The place is great  but the first time you're there you might need help to navigate in which way you should go.

I love this airport  efficient security  helpful people and plenty of places to shop. Trains to and from the city and even over to Sweden to Malmö. Only downside is that the SAS lounge is WAAAYYY over capacity
Gate C5 is a remote gate with bus to the airplane. No seats aviable at the departure gate. Otherwise Copenhagen Airport is ok
Love the Airport but not so many Spotting facilities but there are some Windows. Nice food and supplies services.
Probably my favorite airport in Europe  with a both cosy and stylish atmosphere. The food options are truly fantastic and wifi works very well.

Copenhagen Airport looks beautiful  modern and cozy  and is well connected to the city. However there is still a lot of space for improvements: - There are very few seats  despite a lot of empty space  so in the holiday season you often see people camping on the ground everywhere. I asked bout this  and I was told that seats are not installed not due to lack of space  but not to mess with the "nordic design" of the airport. Form of function at an airport is quite silly. - The WiFi is just crappy. At least they improved it so that you don't need a Danish mobile number anymore to access it. - The StarAlliance lounge is spacious but the food options are really way too limited. - The arrival area is quite disorganized. There is only one main fenced lane just out of customs and is often crowded as people (together with prams and luggages etc.) stop to cheer and hug as they can's wait to get to the open area at the end.

Great airport  I love the wooden floors in some areas and the facilities are well designed and cleaned. I got a bit lost going to my gate but it was probably my fault the train got delayed (outside of the airport) and I was already stressed to get to my gate in time.
Easy to go to and from with the train  especially if you're going to Sweden from there. Moreover  be ahead of time if flying in the afternoon as it's a very busy airport
The airport was very big  and In my opinion should be like Heathrow or Charles De Gaulle  instead of having different areas like Gate A  B  C  divide the terminal into T1  2  3 etc
the airport and Terminal 2 are totally outdated  like from the 80's   service okey  but there are much better airports  even in Europe

Very often transit through here. Decent airport with enough shops to give you something to do while you're waiting. Easy to navigate  enough signing. SAS lounge is a little bit on the small side  but it's enough for a transit. Some of the gate areas are a little small  and not a lot of seating.

Absolutely perfect  super comfortable  great design (note the wooden floor all over the airport)  simple layout  great food and shopping  nice approach btw. ... my personal No1 most favourite airport.
One of the best airports for transits in Europe. Comfortable  easy to get around and with really good restaurants.
Copenhagen airport has everything you need  shops  cafés  wifi. It is very clean  and everything is easy to find. No queues  staff is nice  what else ?!

Everything you need in the airport you can find it like WC  cafeterias  currency exchange  ATM  by the way seats at the arrival hall are somewhat comfortable though sleeping on them can be demanding. Also there are not many people talking in English but it's Scandinavia.

the best airport so far... quick security  nice shops  nice design... everything is perfect. and it's very easy to get to the cit y centre. Also  there are flights to awesome destinations  like the Faroe Islands  Iceland and Greenland!

Good and quite nice airport. The railway station is a part of the terminal. It offers direct connections with Copenhagen  other Danish cities as well as Sweden  especially with such Swedish cities as Malmo and Lund. As regards baggage claim area  it could be improved. After arrival  I was waiting for my baggage longer than I could expect (about 30 minutes). The facilities  food and beverages at this airport are too expensive. So  It is not suitable for those people who earn below the standard level. The toilets were dirty and it was stinking inside them.
Copenhagen Airport is such a pleasent airport. It is so cozy and has a very relaxing feel to it. THe security is very fast and the amount of shops inside is huge and there is something for everyone.
nourriture très chère et de mauvaise qualité. propreté douteuse notamment des wc. les salles attente sont elles aussi quasiment jamais netoyees. Vraiment pas le meilleure endroit pour une escale.

The SAS staff were amazingly helpful! I had forgotten my passport  and only realised with about 1 hour and 30 minutes before the flight. Someone was driving the passport to the airport  and the staff kindly got our bags checked in as standby bags  allowed us to use fast track. An amazing journey  and I made the flight. Thank you!

Very nice and pleasure place  nice and helpful people. Good transportation to the city centre  taxi driver was very nice.We've had no problem with wifi and baggage claim. Kastrup is not far away from the centre  about 15-20 minutes by taxi.

Efficient airport  but often long lines in security. Possibly long distances between gates if transiting. Good selection of shops and restaurants. Sometimes a long wait for luggage. Best way to go downtown is train or metro (depending on destination).

I really like CPH  but the fact that you have to sign up for WIFI is really awful. Get it fixed. The prices in the airport are kind of high as well... Smoking area is great - and quite large  so you don't feel cramped.

Great airport with nice shopping facilities. Try the coffeeshop joe & the juice - the offer a superb thuna sandwich. Free wifi is difficult dur to complex registration; connection hang up several times.

Very good airport  Easy to get to  check-in was fast  security took 3 min  and loads of shops  WiFI was okay. Loads of space to sit down and look on the planes take off and landing while waiting. Overall a very good airport 10/10 would fly here again.

Great airport! The security staff is very friendly but at morning rush around 06.00 its kind of alot people and it took me 15min for waiting .. Its not bad but its always to improve to open more security checks.. Overall its great airport with big taxfree and alot of shopping places. Getting to airport is by bus taxi train metro.

Really nice Airport  not to big  and the transport to and from the airport can be either car  Bus  Metro or Train  and both Metro and Train is located just at the terminal  and there is several departures every hour. The airport is high quality architecture and design (typically Danish design) and since April 2015 the domestic flights are moved to the main terminal reducing the transfer time between international and domestic connection

Copenhagen airport should be one of the best ones where I have ever been. Always there is not any problem at all  times to wait there re fine and services inside there re enough good. To get there is simple and easy  having the possibility to arrive on a fast way by train or metro. Buses  I haven't checked still.

The walks are too long for the amount of flights they actually receive. Many times the flight is delayed from here. Thirdly  I'm always finding difficulties finding a nice quiet spot to have a bite and to work. Most of the places are either overcrowded or way too expensive!

The airport is quite huge but everything is well signposted. There is unlimited free wifi in all terminals (if you sign up for an account). Some places are a bit older than others but okay. The facilities are clean and good. The food and retail shops are cheap and great to have a snack or drink. The baggage forwarding went well  the staff at the second boarding answers every question and provides assistance in every matter.

Security process is poor. Main area is always full  but you can always find a quiet free spot. A lot of shops  bars and cafe  but they are expensive! .All in all a lovely  friendly  good looking airport.

Transportation to/from airport is very well organized  security check is fast. Main area is always full  but always can find free spot. A lot of shops  bars and cafe. Prices - the same as everywhere in Denmark. WiFi is really good.

Easy acces from city. SAS checkin is often a mess with grumpy staff and long queues. Other airlines are more organised and helpful. Security process is poor. Why not open another gate instead of having people managing a line where you can only go one way? And please stop the staff from chewing gum  it is so unprofessional. The terminal is badly designed with long walks  over crowded and full of golf buggies with staff going from one end to the other. Everything very over priced.

Transit in Kopenhagen. Wirklich beeindruckt von diesem schönen Flughafen. Holzfußboden  überall super Einkaufsmöglichkeiten. Wäre gerne länger am Airport geblieben. Wer das gute kostenlose WiFi nutzen will  sollte sich die App des Airport im Vorfeld runterladen.

Apart from the very odd layout of the airport and stands/gates  the airport is brilliant. The most pleasant experience I think I've had in any international airport. The departure area mixed with restaurants and people checking in for their holidays really sets the tone for travelling abroad.

A bit long security queue  but staff is helpful. Lots of self check-in stands  many shopping opportunities  but not at the gates (although there are vending machines). Much walking  as some gates are far away from the security check. There is enough seating at the gate.

Security check personnel rude and numerous times. Why do they scan your luggage if then you have to let them open it and run their dirty hands through it?

However  the staff themselves were friendly enough and there were quite a few facilities inside (prayer rooms  toilets  a museum  cafes).

The airport was less organised than the London airport we had flown from  but this became more apparent when we flew back on the return journey. There were long and slow queues and lots of passport checkpoints. However  the place was fairly clean and airport staff were relatively friendly. Checking in for both international and domestic flights with Egypt Air were pretty simple and efficient. The more thorough security check is done during boarding at each gate  although the gate would not open earlier than it should in order to accommodate the extra time needed. For some reason  there's no money exchange facility inside the international terminal (T2) at all. Something to be mindful of in case anybody wishes to exchange Egyptian pounds back to another currency.

We were 8 people from different countries  all travelling from JNB to FCO. Little signage to direct you to checkin counters. Three levels of security to pass which is great  just give yourself lots of time. Not much in the way of food available. To access WIFI you need a cell phone so they can send you wifi password via text message. Not good if you only have iPad.

Very expensive food and water (4 USD for 0.5 bottle of water). 9 USD for simple sandwich is crazy. Foodcourt was closed.

Departure negatives: Very few electric sockets  poor ventilation in smoking room. Water costs 2 5€ for a small bottle. In front of bathroom door there were standing 2 airport employees (I suppose during their lunch break) and harassing people by asking country they are from and some other "friendly" questions and when traveler leaves the bathroom they point their finger to other hand where's 1€ coin and ask to pay for using bathroom. I told them that I won't pay and just went my ways. Yeah  this is Egypt but I don't think it should be allowed. At least not in airport. Overall  pretty bad experience. Arrival: Everything was quite smooth. Baggage claim was quick.

Water is allowed until the second security station. Lighters can be stay in your bag until the gate (ignore the message that you should dispose the lighter but put it into your bagpack); smocking rooms are available in front of the gates

Not sure  if I should blame HRG or VIE to worst International Airport. I guess  it was Vie. Because terminal structure was much better and coffee was at moderate prices. But Security took off my Lighter (electric  no flame). So smoking got tricky

Lot of local aggressive people standing in front of entrance offering a "help" with the luggage. Screams and driving people like the animals during first security check in entrance to the terminal. Impolite check-in's employees  who are waiting for the bribe (yes bribe  not tips) for choosing the seat. Guard's dismissive attention during the passport control (who are talking the phone). Horrible proper security check-in  with the people who behave like people from the deser and don't know international regulations of permissible items in hand luggage. Looks like want to take something  if they like it  as the next "tips" for sth unknown. I lost aerozol deodorant  antibacterial hand gel (<100 ml)  cause were "flammable"  also paper clip and tie wrap  cause could be dangerous (man looked like see these items for the first time) By a hair's breadth I could lose electronic devices.

Dont dither in this airport. On return flight Security is super tight and all flights to UK go through stringent security. Show ticket at Airport entrance Then Bag scan to get in Terminal Then Check in Then exit visa and passport control checks Then Hand baggage checks Then at the gate everyone is given additional checks   where bags are swabbed for explosives. Well done Egypt for the great security

one of the worst airports. total haos. no toilet paper and tissues but there is person waiting for tip next to toilets. duty free shops runs like a worst bazzar. they just lying to you to sell you something. no advice etc.

Währenddessen ging es an den beiden anderen Schaltern relativ zügig. vorwärts  aber schnell ist was anderes. Viele Passagiere beschwerten sich nach dem unendlich langen Check-in  weil sie von ihren Partner getrennt gesetzt wurden. Freunde von uns wurden ganze 5 Sitzreihen voneinander getrennt platziert - und sie waren kin Einzellfall. Sowas haben wir noch nie erlebt. WiFi hat - wie schon 2015 nicht funktioniert. Schon am Flughafen  war siegende Erhohlung für die Katz.

The terminal area was new and fresh  but egyptions were screaming to hurry up to all of the passengers and the atmosphere was like in the egyption market. Wi-Fi wasn't working. Everything was quick though.

Long security (two checks). A pleasant shopping area but before the second security checks. On the other hand  absolutely no commerce in the departure lounge (only for international flights) which is really disappointing.

Nach Gepäckband muss das Gepäck noch durch eine Sicherheitskontrolle  von dieser gibt es nur eine  sodass mehrere Hundert Leute ihre Koffer nur an einem Band abgeben können  was für lange Wartezeiten sorgt. Das Terminal ist sehr unorganisiert und man braucht erstmal  bis man weiß was zu tun ist.
Watch out when buying a visa on arrival - you wont pay $2 "fee" when doing it through the bank.

One of the best airports in Egypt to arrive to. Very serviceminded staff. Easy to get out off. When departing is RMF only slightly better than other Egyptian airports  with silly checkpoints and annoying controls.

Extremely good security check. Hence took some time to get through to gate area. But worth it. Pizza Hut and coffe shop and some tax free shopping. Only 10 minutes by buss from Coraya Bay. Perfect for spending a vacation!

toilets were horrible and messy  the queues were so slow it took 2 hours to get through 2 security checks and bag drop
Incompetent staff - fight was delayed because they were laughing around not properly boarding people. No info offered during thus time. WiFi didn't work

Fast security controls and check-in  don't need to come too early. Couple hours before departure is fine  you cannot do controls too early. Well guarded airport  before security controls you can only stay seated on a chair and go to the toilets. WiFi is only 15 minutes. As soon as you arrive someone will offer to take your luggage for 5 Euros  just say no and get the cart on your own. Quite thorough and repeated controls  but chill officers. Restaurants and bazar only after the controls  check-in and handbag controls  but they are crazy expensive. Bring your own food at least. In the gate area the AC is at maximum  so you have to cover up in order not to freeze.
A little bit expensive to get a cab from the resort but the well designed architecture of the AirPort makes up for it in the sunrise.
This airport seems to be spared from chaotic Cairo Airport. Well designed and has a lot more capacity to handle more passengers.
Wifi not working (don't receive sms with code). All food services closed. No buses to the city center.
Русофобы ебучие и петушары. На чекине задают странные вопросы и угрожают не пустить на рейс. Пограничники не знают что такое Черногория

Проверяют слишком дотошно  но больше для вида  чем по делу. Фаст лайн реализован просто через человека  который за деньги вас проводит в начало той же очереди  что и все стоят (люди в очереди фыркают  конечно). Вай-фай подключается всего на 15 минут.

17.10.2020 - обычная проверка на входе - обыск босиком  бахилы не предлагают. Через 10 метров 5 человек роются в наших чемоданах постоянно говоря что это в салон нельзя  хотя я им 5 раз сказал что это багаж. Перерыли чемодан полностью  уложить обратно не дают - идите отсюда. После сдачи багажа ещё обыск ручной клади. 15! минут босиком у рамки в ожидании когда закончится перерыв у того кто обыскивает. После ещё 2 обыска. Один охранник забрал хорошую ручку  второй шоколадку. От кондиционера пар изо рта идёт. А перекладывая постоянно вещи и стоя босиком потеешь. После пришлось на борту горячий час за 5 евро покупать. Пятый раз в Египте  третий раз в этом аэропорту. Они одичали. Больше ни ногой в Египет. 10/17/2020 - the usual check at the entrance - a search with bare feet  shoe covers are not offered. After 10 meters  5 people rummage in our suitcases  constantly saying that this is not allowed in the salon  although I told them 5 times that this is luggage. They ransacked the suitcase completely  they do not allow to put it back - get out of here. After checking in your baggage  you will still search your hand luggage fifteen! minutes barefoot at the frame  waiting for the end of the break for the one who searches. After 2 more searches. One guard took a good pen  the other a chocolate bar. Steam from the mouth comes from the air conditioner. And constantly shifting things and standing barefoot sweat. After that I had to buy a hot hour on board for 5 euros. Fifth time in Egypt  third time at this airport. They went wild. No more foot to Egypt. Sorry my english.

Security can often feel rushed and confusing due to the hustle and bustle nature of the airport. All Non-Egyptian flights are required to fill out a departure visa so bare this in mind. The departure lounge was also dated with a few - recently updated - duty free shops. There are around 2 places pick up a drink or a bite to each  which  for the size of the terminal  isn't really enough. Also be aware that the No Smoking rule isn't really obeyed within the terminal.

Buses will take you to the city of Kuressaare in few minutes and are pretty convenient. When it comes to airport there is waiting room before security as well as after security. At security people go one by one. For some reason there is also facility for passport control which I found very funny. Kuressaare is a lovely airport.

The airport is tiny but extremely efficient. It even has a bar for drinks and snacks and should even have a guesthouse. Just expect to encounter the lady serving at the bar also when buying tickets and operating the security control. :) And still  all operations are done properly and according to European rules. For such a small airport it's quite complete  comfortable and very clean. Only minus is that there's no public transport to the airport (only a bus waiting on arrival). Not a problem when the weather's good  because then the 2 kilometer from the city are easily done by foot.

Best airport in the world... Small on the outside but big on the inside  as some would say. Very cozy interior  feels like a true estonian home to be honest. The redesigned ecurity check is just perfect and very quick  and staff are amazingly easy. Good ammount of shops and bars  and my favorite part  a Subway! The lounge and the VIP service (private terminal) are amazing too as I've heard. And of course  clean bathrooms.

Beautiful smaller airport with friendly airport staff. Bunch of inexpensive restaurtans  multiple rooms for smokers  clean restrooms  free wifi and free drinking water tap. Airport is easily reachable by public transport (trams  buses). Everything is alright and for the size of this airport - it definitely deserves 5 stars.

Aeroporto de "pequeno porte" mas que atende bem a região. Não procuramos como sair de transporte público pois chegamos tarde  estávamos cansados e eu ganhei um desconto no Uber. Salvo engano não valia a pena o transporte público.
Tallinn airport is my home airport and it is one of the baltic biggest airport. There is very good spotting locations
Tallinn airport is very beaticul airport and this is my home airport  I like plane spot there are go trip there.

* Most Expensive tax-free shop in the world! Ridiculous! * Best location - close to town * Tram directly to airport * People at airport should take reading classes for announcements - they feel it's a competition who's the fastest at reading them * Nice fast baggage service
The best airport I have ever followed from. Short distances  fast services  good prices  innovative ideas  nice details  quiet and relaxing. It could not be better.

Nice - my home airport after all! But people running the tax free shop are one greedy bunch! Last I checked Estonian income was not on the same level with Luxemburg  Switzerland  Norway or Monaco. Prices in the tax free shop however  indicate otherwise. The most expensive tax free shop in Europe!
First time trying the business lounge. Also last time I'll use that. Rude  and they just wanted to go home. Sorry I guess?!
Small but have their own style  warm feelings. Easy to take taxi or call uber with short distance to go down town.
Poor customer service and "We only speak Estonian" attitude. Wake up TLL airport! No one is going to learn Estonian prior to visiting the country. Learn some English and Russian!

Everything in this airport is completely perfect. Getting to the airport is by tram without any hassle. Security check was fast and the staff was very friendly. The shops after security were very nice and again with very helpful staff. WiFi was excellent. Would definitely fly through this airport again.
Ultra modern airport  excellent service  easy to get to and navigate the airport with clear signage. Friendly security staff too.

Very good and fast transit options to the city center. Nice small  innovative and well built airport.
This really is one of the best airports in Europe (#3 in 2017) You can’t miss anything there. Decent WiFi  easy to access  small and really cozy.

Lovely airport modern quiet lots of seats plenty of places to get a snack/drink very relaxing close to the aircraft so get excellent views for pics etc one of the best terminals I have been in.

Very rude security check people  there's a "no pictures" sign near the security area on the entrance side which couldn't be seen because of the very long security queue. After taking a picture  a female security person yelled "no pictures" in Estonian in what I'd describe as a Soviet era tone to remind you that you're in ex-USSR. With the exception of departure security  a good airport.

The Tallinn airport is very small  tidy  with everything you need for a short - medium flight. It has a good waiting area  fast security checks and for eu flights arrivals it's limited to a fast sight of the docs without any queue. It's located only 4 kms from the city center  making it extremely easy and fast to be reached (about 15 mins) with bus or taxi.

I had a bad experience with the security check. I had forgotten to throw my water bottle previously  so the man started shouting at me in Russian (he just concluded that I'm Russian when I'm not) and when I said "What? Please speak English" he started mocking me for saying what. Awful experience. The airport otherwise is okay.
Nice and compact airport. Great with four sleeper capsules for people wishing to rest a while before flying.
Small but nice airport. Easy to reach by public transportation from the old town. The airport was really empty  although there was some line in the security check.

Getting to the airport is easy  even with public transport (unless you live in the southern part of the city). Automatic check-in terminals are easy and fast to use. Security check is fast  facilities are good and seem fairly new. WiFi is unlimited after accepting the T&C  food is good. Shopping area right after check-in  snacks can be bought from R-Kiosk though the cafés are generally good too.

Best airport ever when you arrive! Calm  quiet  homemade! Interesting design for every gate. Comfortable chairs  fast internet  free equipment. I stayed there for 2 hours just because i like this place. Great!

Small  clean and cozy. The interior design is so not typical for an airport. Estonia starts from the very first step you make in the terminal. The shopping and eating areas are so nice and thoughtfully planned
Nordea Lounge didnt have any food that was even close to be Edible...... Good free WiFi on the whole airport.. Great communication with taxi to airport  close to city center only 10 min with taxi.

Closest free parking to the airport is ~10 minutes walk. Connectivity is good  nice and homey terminal. Security checks may take a while when families travel  but usually it goes fast through them. Too bad they force you to go through tax-free shopping area. For me it is not so pleasant because all of the perfumes bring allergic reaction so I try to rush through it as fast as possible. WiFi works nicely.

I've traveled many time to and from Tallinn  not the best airport  but good enough to accomodate basic needs. This time when flying to Frankfurt Lufthansa managed to park their plane right in the gate and not having any trouble by bus.

Medium size Airport with most flights via gateway. Que to Security can some time be long  more then 10 min. New system with digital Reading of boarding cards is recently installed. Taxi free shops with strong focus on Estonian produced products. Restaurant on both inside and outside of security. Low price Taxi 6-7 Euri to Tallinn City. Bus available from bootom floor  same exit as for rental cars  to City center

Now this airport is so home-like that you can't believe it. The lounges offer couches too. There are TVs around the airport  you can Skype call someone for free  the shops aren't that expensive  the staff is friendly... There is also free wifi all around the airport and it works very well. Overally  you could stay here for quite a while

Small  convenient  close to the centre of Tallin ( Bus nr 2 take You there in about 15 minutes from Viru Centre). Cosy atmosfere inside the departure zone with comfortable seatings. Really nice  efficient airport.

Small but very nice airport. Very easy and fast to get to the city and back to airport. Bus about 1 60€ to the city centre - Taxi from old town to airport ca. 15€. Easy to check in - counter and self-checkin possible  fast security check  no long ways to the gates. Very modern terminal building and nice seats in front of the gates to chill out until boarding. All in all excellent  TLL! :)

It's a small but very efficient airport. Transport is very cheap with regular buses which are scheduled quite frequent. There are not too many check in counters  so it's easy to find your way. I never had to wait in security  it seems pretty efficient and decent. The look and feel of this airport are quite Scandinavian. What I like most about this place is the 'homely' feeling they tried to give to the waiting lounges. Good seats  leather sofas  decoration with museum pieces  some tv channels are on and even fun extras like a business card exchange wall can be found here. There are enough places to eat  drink and shop. All in all  it is quite an enjoyable airport.
